Base/0/b/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Beloved. After defending in battle, you may influence if the attacker took any Trophies. Rivals cannot Ransack the Court when they battle you. Just. When you're scoring Tyrant, gain no Power for second place, and gain Power for second place if you get first place. (Don't get bonus city Power.)
Base/0/b/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Elder

Base/0/b/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Attuned. When you Copy or Pivot in order to tax, you gain 1 Psionic along with the taxed resource. Cryptic. In setup, place agents on your Material and Fuel Outrage slots on your player board.
Base/0/b/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Mystic

Base/0/b/piece_0_2.jpg.description: Insatiable. When you Copy or Pivot in order to tax, you gain 1 Fuel along with the taxed resource. Lavish. After scoring, you must discard all of your Fuel if the Tycoon ambition was scored.
Base/0/b/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Fuel-Drinker

Base/0/b/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Ambitious. When you declare an ambition, you may gain any resource. (You must declare the ambition before taking any actions, including Prelude actions from resources.) Callow. You can only tax Loyal cities if you control them.
Base/0/b/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Upstart

Base/0/b/piece_1_0.jpg.description: Committed. In battle, you collect 2 extra dice. Disorganized. You cannot move more than 2 ships at a time.
Base/0/b/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Rebel

Base/0/b/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Tactical. When you Copy or Pivot to battle, you may move before or after you battle. (You may do this even if you spent a Weapon to let you battle.) Violent. When you're scoring Empath, gain no Power for second place, and gain Power for second place if you get first place. (Don't get bonus city Power.)
Base/0/b/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Warrior

Base/0/b/piece_1_2.jpg.description: Charismatic. When you Copy or Pivot to secure a card, you may influence before or after you secure. Generous. To declare an ambition, you must give 1 Guild card to your Rival with the least Power.
Base/0/b/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Feastbringer

Base/0/b/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Bold. When you declare an ambition, you may influence any number of cards in the Court once. Paranoid. You cannot secure Guild cards with only 1 Loyal agent on them. (You can secure Vox cards as normal.) Ignore this if you Ransack the Court.
Base/0/b/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Demagogue

Base/0/x/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Learned. After setup, gain 2 extra lore cards—draw 5 lore, keep 2, and scrap the other 3 (returning them to the box). Academic. When you're scoring Tycoon, gain no Power for second place, and gain Power for second place if you get first place. (Don't get bonus city Power.)
Base/0/x/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Archivist

Base/0/x/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Ruthless. Once per turn, when you tax any city or build a ship at a starport, you may hit the building to take that action again with it. If you destroy a Loyal city, you Provoke Outrage. (If you destroy a Rival city, Provoke Outrage and Ransack the Court.) Hated. In setup, scrap 2 Loyal ships and 3 Loyal agents. (Return them to the box.)
Base/0/x/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Overseer

Base/0/x/piece_0_2.jpg.description: Tricky. When attacking in battle, you may reroll raid dice up to the number of different resources you have. Wary. When attacking in battle, you cannot collect more assault dice than skirmish dice.
Base/0/x/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Corsair

Base/0/x/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Connected. When you declare an ambition that's not declared, draw and secure the top card of the Court deck. (You can't use its Prelude action now.) Influential. When you Copy or Pivot to influence, you may influence twice. Proud. When scoring, you only gain Power if you get first place (not tied).
Base/0/x/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Noble

Base/0/x/piece_1_0.jpg.description: Decentralized. In setup, scrap your 2 leftmost cities from your player board. Inspiring. In systems with Loyal ships, you may tax Rival cities ignoring control, and you may tax empty building slots like Loyal cities. Principled. You cannot tax Loyal cities. Remember, if you have no buildings on the map, Rivals can raid your ships!
Base/0/x/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Anarchist

Base/0/x/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Mythic. After you tax a city, you may place 1 resource you have over the planet's resource icon. From now on, its planet type is the placed resource, and its planet type cannot be changed again with Mythic. Ancient. You cannot Catapult move from starports, but you can Catapult move from gates.
Base/0/x/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Shaper

Base/0/x/piece_1_2.jpg.description: Firebrand. When you Copy or Pivot in order to tax, you gain 1 Weapon along with the taxed resource. Irregular. If a Rival attacks you in battle and rolls ◯, they do not take hits from fresh defending ships. Instead, they take 1 hit per Weapon icon you have from cards and resources, then you discard 1 Weapon resource you have.
Base/0/x/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Agitator

Base/0/x/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Resilient. After any battle with you, repair 1 Loyal ship in the battle system per starport on the map you control. (Even Rival ones!) Greedy. In setup, place an agent on your Material Outrage slot.
Base/0/x/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Quartermaster

Base/1/b/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Summon (Build): Once per turn, build 1 ship at any city you control. Yes, even Rival cities you control!
Base/1/b/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Tool-Priests

Base/1/b/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Fire Rifles (Battle): Like in a battle, choose a system and collect skirmish dice for fresh Loyal ships there, choose a defender in an adjacent system, roll the collected dice, and dead 1 hit to the defender per * rolled. (Hit ships before buildings.)
Base/1/b/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Galactic Rifles

Base/1/b/piece_0_2.jpg.description: When you move fresh Loyal ships, you may move any of them one more time. (Damaged ships can move with them once, but won't move one more time. Resolve this after all Catapult moves.)
Base/1/b/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Sprinter Drives

Base/1/b/piece_0_3.jpg.description: When you defend in battle, add 1 ◯ to the attacker's roll if they rolled any number of assault dice.
Base/1/b/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Mirror Plating

Base/1/b/piece_0_4.jpg.description: You always build ships fresh. When defending in battle, the attacker cannot collect raid dice if you have a fresh defending starport.
Base/1/b/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Hidden Harbors

Base/1/b/piece_0_5.jpg.description: When attacking in battle, if all attacking ships are fresh at the start of the battle, you ignore 1 ◯ you roll.
Base/1/b/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Signal Breaker

Base/1/b/piece_0_6.jpg.description: After you battle as the attacker, repair 1 Loyal attacking ship.
Base/1/b/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Repair Drones

Base/1/b/piece_1_0.jpg.description: You may build starports on gates, max 1 per gate. When Rival ships take a move into a gate you control with a fresh Loyal starport, capture 1 agent of that Rival.
Base/1/b/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Gate Ports

Base/1/b/piece_1_1.jpg.description: You may build cities on planets outside their building slots, max 1 per planet.This costs 1 resource of the planet type.
Base/1/b/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Cloud Cities

Base/1/b/piece_1_2.jpg.description: Nurture (Build): Tax a Loyal city. Prune (Repair): Replace a Loyal starport with a Loyal city or vice versa.
Base/1/b/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Living Structures

Base/1/b/piece_1_3.jpg.description: You may build cities on gates, max 1 per gate. A gate city's type matches all cities in its cluster. (Players may tax it for 1 resource of any of those cities. If it is destroyed, Provoke Outrage of all those city types.)
Base/1/b/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Gate Stations

Base/1/b/piece_1_4.jpg.description: When defending in battle, before the attacker collects dice, they must take 1 hit if any Loyal defending ships are fresh.
Base/1/b/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Railgun Arrays

Base/1/b/piece_1_5.jpg.description: This card has one resource slot with a raid cost of 4 <key>s. (You can hold one resource here. It adds to your ambitions. You can spend it.)
Base/1/b/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Ancient Holdings

Base/1/b/piece_1_6.jpg.description: When attacking in battle, after rolling dice, you may reroll up 1 assault die for each fresh Loyal attacking ship.
Base/1/b/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Seeker Torpedoes

Base/1/x/piece_0_0.jpg.description: When defending in battle, before the attacker collects dice, you may move any number of fresh Loyal ships from systems adjacent to the battle system into it.
Base/1/x/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Predictive Sensors

Base/1/x/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Guide (Move): Move any number of any ships (even if not Loyal) from a system with a fresh Loyal starport to an adjacent system, or vice versa, ignoring move modifiers.
Base/1/x/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Force Beams

Base/1/x/piece_0_2.jpg.description: When attacking in battle, if there are no defending buildings, you may collect up to 1 raid die. (This is not an extra die. Follow the limit of 1 die per ship.)
Base/1/x/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Raider Exosuits

Base/1/x/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Martyr (Move): Destroy 1 fresh Loyal ship on the map to destroy 1 ship that is not Loyal in its system, taking it as a Trophy. (Your Loyal ship does not become a Trophy.)
Base/1/x/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Survival Overrides

Base/1/x/piece_0_4.jpg.description: While Empath is declared, if you roll any dice (even outside battle), you may reroll any number of them once. Prelude: You may discard this to clear your Psionic Outrage.
Base/1/x/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Empath's Vision

Base/1/x/piece_0_5.jpg.description: While Empath is declared, you may tax any cities, and build ships and Catapult move with any starports, like they are Loyal. (Don't take Captives. Build ships damaged in blockaded systems.)
Base/1/x/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Empath's Bond

Base/1/x/piece_0_6.jpg.description: While Keeper is declared, Rivals cannot steal resources from you that are the same as resources they have already. Prelude: You may discard this to clear your Relic Outrage.
Base/1/x/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Keeper's Trust

Base/1/x/piece_1_0.jpg.description: While Keeper is declared, Rivals cannot steal Guild cards from you while you have any resources of the same type. (If you have both Relics and Sworn Guardians, neither can be stolen!)
Base/1/x/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Keeper's Solidarity

Base/1/x/piece_1_1.jpg.description: While Warlord is declared, you may tax cities that you already taxed this turn. Prelude: You may discard this to clear your Weapon Outrage.
Base/1/x/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Warlord's Cruelty

Base/1/x/piece_1_2.jpg.description: While Warlord is declared, you may spend Trophies in your Prelude, returning them, to influence once for each.
Base/1/x/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Warlord's Terror

Base/1/x/piece_1_3.jpg.description: While Tyrant is declared, you may spend Captives in your Prelude, returning them, to secure once for each. Prelude: You may discard this to clear your Weapon Outrage.
Base/1/x/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Tyrant's Ego

Base/1/x/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Annex (Build): While Tyrant is declared, replace any city or starport you control with a Loyal city or starport, respectively. (Cities return to player boards.)
Base/1/x/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Tyrant's Authority

Base/1/x/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Prelude: While Tycoon is declared, before taking any other actions, you may discard all of your Material and Fuel to declare exactly 1 undeclared ambition. Do not place the zero marker. (You may do this just after declaring Tycoon.)
Base/1/x/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Tycoon's Ambition

Base/1/x/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Prelude: While Tycoon is declared, you may discard any number of Material and Fuel to gain 1 resource for each. Prelude: You may discard this to clear your Material and Fuel Outrage.
Base/1/x/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Tycoon's Charm

Base/2/piece_0_0.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Material. You ignore Outrage when spending Material for its Prelude action.
Base/2/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Loyal Engineers

Base/2/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Manufacture (Build): Gain 1 Material. Prelude: You may discard this to gain Material up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Material supply empties, steal the Material instead.
Base/2/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Mining Interest

Base/2/piece_0_2.jpg.description: You keep the Material supply on here. (You add it to Tycoon but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Material. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Material.
Base/2/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Material Cartel

Base/2/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Administration card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Base/2/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Admin Union

Base/2/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Construction card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Base/2/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Construction Union

Base/2/piece_0_5.jpg.description: You keep the Fuel supply on here. (You add it to Tycoon but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Fuel. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Fuel.
Base/2/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Fuel Cartel

Base/2/piece_0_6.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Fuel. You ignore Outrage when spending Fuel for its Prelude action.
Base/2/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Loyal Pilots

Base/2/piece_1_0.jpg.description: When you battle in a gate, you may collect 2 more dice. Prelude: You may discard this to place 1 ship in each gate (unless out of play).
Base/2/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Gatekeepers

Base/2/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Synthesize (Build): Gain 1 Fuel. Prelude: You may discard this to gain Fuel up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Fuel supply empties, steal the Fuel instead.
Base/2/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Shipping Interest

Base/2/piece_1_2.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Mobilization card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Base/2/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Spacing Union

Base/2/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Aggression card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Base/2/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Arms Union

Base/2/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Pressgang (Build): Return any number of your Captives to gain any 1 resource for each. Execute (Influence): Move any number of your Captives to your Trophies. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Base/2/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Prison Wardens

Base/2/piece_1_5.jpg.description: After you roll in battle, you may reroll a number of skirmish dice up to your total Weapon icons from resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Base/2/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Skirmishers

Base/2/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Abduct (Battle): Capture all Rival agents from a card in the Court that has fewer Rival agents than your total Weapon icons from resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Base/2/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Court Enforcers

Base/2/piece_2_0.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Weapons. You ignore Outrage when spending Weapons for their Prelude action. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Base/2/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Loyal Marines

Base/2/piece_2_1.jpg.description: Prelude: Before any other actions, you may discard this to seize the initiative. You can't seize the initiative if you have it or if it's already seized this round!
Base/2/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Lattice Spies

Base/2/piece_2_2.jpg.description: When you declare an ambition, look at a Rival's hand. You may swap 1 card with them. Prelude: You may discard this and any number of cards from your hand. Draw the same number of cards (including Farseers) from the bottom of the action discard pile.
Base/2/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Farseers

Base/2/piece_2_3.jpg.description: When you declare Keeper or Empath, do not place the zero marker.
Base/2/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Secret Order

Base/2/piece_2_4.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Psionic. You ignore Outrage when spending Psionic for its Prelude action.
Base/2/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Loyal Empaths

Base/2/piece_2_5.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to steal a Guild card or resource.
Base/2/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Silver-Tongues

Base/2/piece_2_6.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Relics. You ignore Outrage when spending Relics for their Prelude action.
Base/2/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Loyal Keepers

Base/2/piece_3_0.jpg.description: Rivals cannot steal your resources and other Guild cards. (In battle they can steal this first and then spend <key>s) If this card is stolen, bury it. (Place it on the bottom of the Court deck.)
Base/2/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Sworn Guardians

Base/2/piece_3_1.jpg.description: Trade (Tax): Choose a Rival city you control. Swap 1 resource with that Rival—take a resource of that city type from them, and give them a resource they don't have. Prelude: You may discard this to gain 1 Material, 1 Fuel, and 1 Weapon.
Base/2/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Elder Broker

Base/2/piece_3_2.jpg.description: Prelude: Once per turn, you may discard 1 resource to gain 1 Relic.
Base/2/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Relic Fence

Base/2/piece_3_3.jpg.description: When you Surpass or Pivot, before taking any actions, you may declare the ambition on your played card if an ambition has not been declared yet this round. Do not place the zero marker.
Base/2/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Galactic Bards

Base/2/piece_3_4.jpg.description: When Secured: Choose a cluster on the map. You place 1 ship in each system of that cluster. Discard this card.
Base/2/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Mass Uprising

Base/2/piece_3_5.jpg.description: When Secured: You may declare any ambition. (An ambition marker must be Available.) Discard this card.
Base/2/piece_3_5.jpg.name: Populist Demands

Base/2/piece_3_6.jpg.description: When Secured: You may choose an Outrage type. Each player (even you) must Provoke Outrage of that type. Discard this card.
Base/2/piece_3_6.jpg.name: Outrage Spreads

Base/2/piece_4_0.jpg.description: When Secured: You may return any one city you control to its player board. If you do, you may seize the initiative. Shuffle this card into the Court deck. You can't seize the initiative if you have it or if it's already seized this round!
Base/2/piece_4_0.jpg.name: Song of Freedom

Base/2/piece_4_1.jpg.description: When Secured: You may steal 1 Guild card from a Rival. Shuffle all Guild cards from the Court discard pile into the Court deck. Discard Guild Struggle.
Base/2/piece_4_1.jpg.name: Guild Struggle

Base/2/piece_4_2.jpg.description: When Secured: Draw 1 action card from the bottom of the action discard pile. Discard Call to Action.
Base/2/piece_4_2.jpg.name: Call to Action

Campaign/0/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Manufacture (Build): Gain 1 Material. Prelude: You may discard this to gain Material up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Material supply empties, steal the Material instead.
Campaign/0/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Mining Interest

Campaign/0/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Construction card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/0/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Construction Union

Campaign/0/piece_0_2.jpg.description: When you battle in a gate, you may collect 2 more dice. Prelude: You may discard this to place 1 ship in each gate.
Campaign/0/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Gatekeepers

Campaign/0/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Synthesize (Build): Gain 1 Fuel. Prelude: You may discard this to gain Fuel up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Fuel supply empties, steal the Fuel instead.
Campaign/0/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Shipping Interest

Campaign/0/piece_0_4.jpg.description: After you roll in battle, you may reroll a number of skirmish dice up to your total Weapon icons from resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/0/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Skirmishers

Campaign/0/piece_0_5.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Aggression card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/0/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Arms Union

Campaign/0/piece_0_6.jpg.description: Prelude: Before any other actions, you may discard this to seize the initiative. You can't seize the initiative if you have it or if it's already seized this round!
Campaign/0/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Lattice Spies

Campaign/0/piece_1_0.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to steal a Guild card or resource.
Campaign/0/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Silver-Tongues

Campaign/0/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Rivals cannot steal your resources and other Guild cards. (In battle they can steal this first and then spend <key>.) If this card is stolen, bury it. (Place it on the bottom of the Court deck.)
Campaign/0/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Sworn Guardians

Campaign/0/piece_1_2.jpg.description: Trade (Tax): Choose a Rival city you control. Swap 1 resource with that Rival—take a resource of that city type from them, and give them a resource they don't have. Prelude: You may discard this to gain 1 Material, 1 Fuel, and 1 Weapon.
Campaign/0/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Elder Broker

Campaign/0/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Crisis: Return the declared ambition marker of highest Power to Available Ambitions. When Secured: You may declare any ambition. (An ambition marker must be Available.) Bury this card.
Campaign/0/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Populist Demands

Campaign/0/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Flip the Imperial Council to In Session. Move agents from here to it. Bury this card. When Secured: Flip the Imperial Council to In Session. You may move any Loyal agents from here to other Court cards (even the Council). Bury this card.
Campaign/0/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Council Intrigue

Campaign/0/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player with more Guild cards than agents in their supply must discard Guild cards until their Guild cards equal their agents. Bury this card. (Count protected Guild cards but don't discard them.) When Secured: Rivals resolve the Crisis.
Campaign/0/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Diplomatic Fiasco

Campaign/0/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Crisis: The player with the most Power must replace a Loyal city with a Free city. When Secured: You may replace any Loyal or Rival city you control with a Free city. If you do, you may seize the initiative. Shuffle this card into the Court deck.
Campaign/0/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Song of Freedom

Campaign/0/piece_2_0.jpg.description: Crisis: Repair all Blight in the rolled cluster. Place 1 damaged Blight at each planet of the rolled symbol with no Blight. Bury this card. When Secured: You may destroy all damaged Blight in a cluster with Loyal pieces. Bury this card.
Campaign/0/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Blight Looms

Campaign/1/Fate1.jpg.description:
Hold together a fragmenting empire while navigating fractious court politics.

Your path to power:
I. Assert your authority by winning ambitions as the First Regent.
II. Extend the Empire's control and fill the Imperial Trust.
III. Solidify the Empire's control over Outlaw cities and fill the Imperial Trust as the First Regent.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/1/Fate1.jpg.name: Steward

Campaign/1/back_0_4.jpg.description:
Steward – Act III. Entrench the Empire in the Reach.

<target> You're a Regent and the Empire controls all Outlaw cities or there are no Outlaws.
<target> You're the First Regent and the Imperial Trust has more resources than the current Chapter.
Campaign/1/back_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/1/back_2_0.jpg.description:
Steward – Act II. <timer> 22.

Expand the Empire. At the end of each chapter, if you're the First Regent, advance <timer> 1 for each cluster where the Empire controls any systems.

Ensure the Emperor's Tribute. At the end of each chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each resource in the First Regent's Imperial Trust.
» You advance this way even if you're not First Regent.
Campaign/1/back_2_0.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/1/back_3_4.jpg.description:
Steward – Act I. <timer> 14–20 (2 players: 20 <timer> / 3 players: 17 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Consolidate Your Imperial Power. Each time you win an ambition while you're the First Regent, advance <timer> 1 for each Power you gain from it.
» Count bonus Power from city slots uncovered on your player board. For example, if you gain 5 Power from winning an ambition and gain 5 bonus city Power, advance your objective marker 10 spaces.
Campaign/1/back_3_4.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/1/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
A Policy of Peace.
1. Collect Demand: Take 1 Relic or Psionic from each Regent, even you, and from each Outlaw with a Loyal city or Flagship controlled by the Empire. Place it in the Imperial Trust on your First Regent tile. If they have none, take 1 agent from their supply as a Favor. If a Regent has no agents, you may make them an Outlaw.
2. Imperial Action: Each Regent gains double their bonus city Power on uncovered city slots.
Campaign/1/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Govern with Authority (A Policy of Peace)

Campaign/1/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
A Policy of Escalation.
1. Collect Demand: Take 1 Weapon, Fuel, or Material from each Regent, even you, and from each Outlaw with a Loyal city or Flagship controlled by the Empire. Place it in the Imperial Trust on your First Regent tile. If they have none, take 1 agent from their supply as a Favor. If a Regent has no agents, you may make them an Outlaw.
2. Imperial Action: Place 4 fresh Imperial ships in an Empire-controlled system. If there are none, place them at a Loyal starport.
Campaign/1/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Govern with Authority (A Policy of Escalation)

Campaign/1/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
A Policy of War.
1. Collect Demand: Take 1 Trophy or Captive, but not Imperial or Regent pieces, from each Rival Regent and from each Outlaw with a Loyal city or Flagship controlled by the Empire. If they have none, take 1 agent from their supply as a Favor. If a Regent has no agents, you may make them an Outlaw.
2. Imperial Action: Give 1 Weapon and any 1 resource to each Regent from the supply. If the supply is empty, give it from the Imperial Trust.
Campaign/1/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Govern with Authority (A Policy of War)

Campaign/1/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
The First Regent must place the Sponsored token on an undeclared ambition box. (If it's already placed, they may keep it on its current ambition box.) Then, they may add 1 resource they have to the Imperial Trust.

When a player declares the Sponsored ambition, they must take 1 resource from the Imperial Trust, gain the Power for second place on the placed ambition marker, and return the Sponsored token to the First Regent tile. If they are an Outlaw, they become a Regent.
Campaign/1/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Imperial Sponsor

Campaign/1/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Steward – Act III.
1. Add Imperial Sponsor (23) to the rules booklet.
2. Place the Sponsored token on the First Regent tile.
3. Scrap the 3 Govern the Imperial Reach cards (<diamond>01–03) from the rules booklet and add the 3 Govern with Authority cards (24–26) to it.
4. The First Regent chooses which Govern with Authority card to place on top of the stack. Explain that Outlaws are now subject to the Imperial Demand if the Empire controls a city or Flagship of theirs.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/1/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/1/piece_0_5.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to declare any ambition.
Campaign/1/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Lesser Regent 4

Campaign/1/piece_0_6.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to declare any ambition.
Campaign/1/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Lesser Regent 3

Campaign/1/piece_1_0.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may resolve the Crisis in any cluster. Bury or discard this card.
Campaign/1/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 2

Campaign/1/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may resolve the Crisis in any cluster. Bury or discard this card.
Campaign/1/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 1

Campaign/1/piece_1_2.jpg.description: When you Surpass or Pivot, before taking any actions, you may declare the ambition on your played card if an ambition has not been declared yet this round. Do not place the zero marker.
Campaign/1/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Galactic Bards

Campaign/1/piece_1_3.jpg.description: When you secure a Guild card, you may flip the Imperial Council to In Session or you may influence the Imperial Council if it is already In Session.
Campaign/1/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Council Insiders

Campaign/1/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Each Imperial ship hits 1 ship of each Outlaw in its system, fresh ships first. Bury this card. When Secured: You may resolve the Crisis in one cluster you choose, or you may hit all Imperial ships in a cluster you choose. Bury this card.
Campaign/1/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Navy Reprisal

Campaign/1/piece_1_5.jpg.description:
Steward – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add Navy Reprisal (15), Council Insiders (16), and Galactic Bards (17) to the Court deck.
2. Scrap the 2 Imperial Defectors (18–19) and 2 Lesser Regents (20–22).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Navy Reprisal (15), Council Insiders (16), and Galactic Bards (17).
2. Add the 2 Imperial Defectors (18–19) and 2 Lesser Regents (20–22) to the Court deck.
Campaign/1/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/1/piece_1_6.jpg.description:
Resolve the effects of Edict 03—Govern the Imperial Reach, or Govern with Authority in Act III—as follows, then skip Edict 03 itself.

First, Collect Demand: The First Regent collects the Demand.
Second, Take Action: The Regent with the most starports built may take the Imperial Action. On a tie, skip this.
Third, Change Policy: The Regent with the most cities built may change the Policy of Edict 03. On a tie, skip this.

The conditions "most starports built" and "most cities built" do include cities and starports on Flagship boards.
Campaign/1/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Imperial Quorum

Campaign/1/piece_2_0.jpg.description:
Steward – Act II.
1. Add Imperial Quorum (13) to the Edicts.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/1/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/1/piece_2_1.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to declare any ambition.
Campaign/1/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Lesser Regent 2

Campaign/1/piece_2_2.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to declare any ambition.
Campaign/1/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Lesser Regent 1

Campaign/1/piece_2_3.jpg.description: Crisis: If <triangle> was rolled, all Regents become Outlaws, hit each Imperial ship on the map, and discard this card. When Secured: You may take any Loyal agents from here and place them on other Court cards. Bury this card.
Campaign/1/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Empire Falls

Campaign/1/piece_2_4.jpg.description: Blight Crises do not happen in systems controlled by the Empire. (That is, in systems with any fresh Imperial ships.)
Campaign/1/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Imperial Protectors

Campaign/1/piece_2_5.jpg.description: When attacking an Outlaw in battle, you may reroll assault dice up to your total Weapons resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/1/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Hunter Squads

Campaign/1/piece_2_6.jpg.description: Bury this if you're an Outlaw. When you tax an Outlaw's city, you may take the resource from their player board.
Campaign/1/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Tax Collectors

Campaign/1/piece_3_0.jpg.description: Crisis: Flip the Imperial Council to In Session. Move agents from here to it. Bury this card. When Secured: Flip the Imperial Council to In Session. You may move any Loyal agents from here to other Court cards (even the Council). Bury this card.
Campaign/1/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Council Intrigue

Campaign/1/piece_3_1.jpg.description:
Steward – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add Council Intrigue (05), Tax Collectors (06), and Hunter Squads (07) to the Court deck.
2. Add Imperial Protectors (08) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
3. Scrap Empire Falls (09) and 2 Lesser Regents (10–11).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Council Intrigue (05), Tax Collectors (06), Hunter Squads (07), and Imperial Protectors (08).
2. Add Empire Falls (09) and 2 Lesser Regents (10–11) to the Court deck.
3. Add Dealmakers (03) to the Court deck.
Campaign/1/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/1/piece_3_2.jpg.description: Bargain (Secure): Choose a Court card that a Rival could secure. They secure it but return agents they would capture. You influence once for each agent they had on the card, then you secure once.
Campaign/1/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Dealmakers

Campaign/1/piece_3_3.jpg.description: Bury this if you're an Outlaw. You may tax any cities controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal, ignoring the Empire's Presence and Truce Laws. (Don't take Captives.)
Campaign/1/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Imperial Authority

Campaign/1/piece_3_4.jpg.description:
Steward – Act I.
1. Gain Imperial Authority (02) and Dealmakers (03).
2. Gain the First Regent tile.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/1/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/10/Fate10.jpg.description:
Division breeds chaos. Order demands that we follow one banner, think with one mind.

Your path to power:
II. Subdue your Rivals and plant Banners of a new order. Abandon your buildings for a Flagship, lose some of your fleet forever, and also replenish your fleet.
III. Maintain many Banners on the map. Rivals can gain Power by holding them as Trophies.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/10/Fate10.jpg.name: Hegemon

Campaign/10/back_0_1.jpg.description:
Hegemon – Act III. Form One Body, One Mind.

<target> No Rival won the Tyrant ambition.
<target> More Banners are on the map than the current Chapter.
Campaign/10/back_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/10/back_1_4.jpg.description:
Hegemon – Act II. <timer> 14.

Plant Banners of the New Order.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Banner on the map.
If you won any number of ambitions, advance <timer> 1 for each Banner on the map again.
» For example, if you win 3 ambitions and 2 Banners are on the map, you advance 4.
» Your Planting Banners card explains how to place Banners.
Campaign/10/back_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/10/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
Pacify (Secure): Capture all damaged ships you control except Loyal ones.
When Captives are returned in scoring, you may keep 1 Captive per Banner that you control or is uncontrolled.
Campaign/10/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Happy Hosts

Campaign/10/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Hegemon – Act III.
1. Gain the Happy Hosts card (12).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/10/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/10/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Scrap this if no Banners are on the map.
Crisis: Bury this card.
When Secured: You may take any Banners you control as Trophies. Discard this card.
Campaign/10/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Against Hegemony 2

Campaign/10/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Scrap this if no Banners are on the map.
Crisis: Bury this card.
When Secured: You may take any Banners you control as Trophies. Discard this card.
Campaign/10/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Against Hegemony 1

Campaign/10/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Pressgang (Build): Return any number of your Captives to gain any 1 resource for each.
Execute (Influence): Move any number of your Captives to your Trophies.
Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/10/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Prison Wardens 2

Campaign/10/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Pressgang (Build): Return any number of your Captives to gain any 1 resource for each.
Execute (Influence): Move any number of your Captives to your Trophies.
Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/10/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Prison Wardens 1

Campaign/10/piece_0_6.jpg.description: Manipulate (Influence): Choose a Rival that you have a Captive of. Peek at their hand, take 1 card from it, then give any card from your hand.
Campaign/10/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Mind Managers

Campaign/10/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Hegemon – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Mind Managers (06).
2. Add 2 Prison Wardens (07–08) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap the 2 Against Hegemony cards (09–10).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Mind Managers (06).
2. Add 2 Prison Wardens (07–08) and 2 Against Hegemony (09–10) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap all Banner tokens that are not on the map.
Campaign/10/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/10/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
You may move between planets with Banners as if they were adjacent.
Call Spirits (Tax): Once per turn, gain 2 resources of a planet with a Banner.
Call Bodies (Build): Once per turn, place 2 fresh ships at a Banner.
Campaign/10/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Song of the Banner

Campaign/10/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
The Hegemon can place a Banner at their Flagship's planet if it has no non-Loyal ships or buildings.
Building. Banners can be harmed and repaired like buildings. (They can't be raided. Blight destroys them in the Intermission if no ships are in their system.)
Valuable. When scoring Warlord, gain 2 extra Power for each Banner you have in your Trophies. (Gain this Power even if you didn't gain Power from placing in Warlord.)
Campaign/10/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Banners of Hegemony

Campaign/10/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you don't have a Flagship.
Banner tokens are your Loyal pieces.
Prelude: You may place a Banner at your Flagship's planet if it has no Banners and no ships or buildings that are not Loyal. (You can do this multiple times.)
Campaign/10/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Planting Banners

Campaign/10/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Hegemon – Act II.
1. You gain a Flagship—take a Flagship board and set it up as described on the board back.
2. Place 1 fresh starport each on your Defense Array and Slipstream Drive Upgrades.
3. Replenish your fleet—if you have fewer than 6 Loyal ships on the map other than your Flagship, place ships at your Flagship until you have 6.
4. Scrap any 4 Loyal ships.
5. Gain the Planting Banners card (02). Collect the 8 Banner tokens.
6. Add the Banners of Hegemony card (03) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
7. Gain Song of the Banner (04).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/10/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/11/Fate11.jpg.description:
There are simply too many of us.

Your path to power:
II. Collect the Hammer Fragments to repair the Planet Hammer, then break a planet. Abandon your buildings for a Flagship.
III. Fill the Reach with Outrage and destroy buildings by breaking planets. Rivals can clear Outrage and gain resources by resettling Refugees.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/11/Fate11.jpg.name: Planet Breaker

Campaign/11/back_0_0.jpg.description:
Planet Breaker – Act III. Break the Worlds of the Reach.
No Rival wins the Warlord ambition and...

<target> Every player has Outrage.
<target> More buildings are on the Breaking Worlds card than the current Chapter, or there are no buildings on the map.
Campaign/11/back_0_0.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/11/back_1_2.jpg.description:
This action harms all buildings on the planet.
In turn order, each Rival may choose to battle you at the planet with the Hammer token.
After these battles, if you control the planet, flip the Hammer token to its Broken World side and resolve this.
1. All players Provoke Outrage of this planet type.
2. Untuck the Refugee card (05–09) of this planet type from here. Place it on top of the Court deck.
3. Place all buildings from the planet on here.
4. Scrap all Guild cards in the Court of the planet type. Return all agents from them.
Planets that are Broken Worlds have no building slots.
Campaign/11/back_1_2.jpg.name: Breaking Worlds

Campaign/11/back_1_4.jpg.description:
Planet Breaker – Act II. <timer> 12.

Repair the Planet Hammer.
Advance <timer> 1 for each Hammer Fragment you take from the map.

Crush a Planet into Dust.
Advance <timer> 6 when you use the Planet Hammer to place a Broken World token on a planet.
» You gain the Hammer of Worlds once you collect all 6 Hammer Fragments.
Campaign/11/back_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/11/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
Planet Breaker – Act III.
1. Place pieces that were on your Breaking Worlds card in Act II back on your Breaking Worlds card (03).
2. Remind everyone that placing a Broken World token on a planet will Provoke Outrage for all players of that planet type.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/11/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/11/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Crisis: Roll the event die again. If you roll the same symbol, place a Broken World token on the rolled planet. Resolve the Breaking Worlds card.
When Secured: Roll 1 skirmish die per agent here. If you roll at least 3 <hit>, gain 3 Power, draw the bottom card from the action discard pile, and bury this card. If not, this card and agents on it remain.
Campaign/11/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Planet-Eater Loose

Campaign/11/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Planet Breaker – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Scrap Planet-Eater Loose (11).
2. Record all pieces that are on your Breaking Worlds card (03). (They stay on it in Act III.)

If you failed your objective:
1. Add Planet-Eater Loose (11) to the Court deck.
2. Resolve the text under "If you have all 6 Hammer Fragments" on the Hammer Fragments card (03) if you haven't already.
3. Place the Breaking Worlds card (03) and 6 Broken Worlds tokens in the supply. (They are used by the Planet-Eater Loose card in Act III.)
4. Scrap your Planet Hammer (04).
Campaign/11/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/11/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you have the Planet Hammer.
Resettle (Build): Build a Free city on a Psionic planet you control. Clear your Psionic Outrage and gain Psionic up to your number of empty resource slots. Tuck this card under the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Heartworld Refugees

Campaign/11/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you have the Planet Hammer.
Resettle (Build): Build a Free city on a Relic planet you control. Clear your Relic Outrage and gain Relics up to your number of empty resource slots. Tuck this card under the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Lostworld Refugees

Campaign/11/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you have the Planet Hammer.
Resettle (Build): Build a Free city on a Weapon planet you control. Clear your Weapon Outrage and gain Weapons up to your number of empty resource slots. Tuck this card under the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Deadworld Refugees

Campaign/11/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you have the Planet Hammer.
Resettle (Build): Build a Free city on a Fuel planet you control. Clear your Fuel Outrage and gain Fuel up to your number of empty resource slots. Tuck this card under the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Blazeworld Refugees

Campaign/11/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you have the Planet Hammer.
Resettle (Build): Build a Free city on a Material planet you control. Clear your Material Outrage and gain Material up to your number of empty resource slots. Tuck this card under the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Forgeworld Refugees

Campaign/11/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Prepare (Build): Place a Hammer token at a planet you control. If one is already on the map, return it to your supply first.
Break World (Battle): If you did not Prepare this turn, resolve the Breaking Worlds card.
Campaign/11/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Planet Hammer

Campaign/11/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Repair Hammer (Repair): Take 1 Hammer Fragment token you control and place it on this card. Place 1 fresh ship at the planet you took it from.
If you have all 6 Hammer Fragments on this card:
1. Untuck the Planet Hammer card from here and take it. Hammer Fragment tokens are now known as Hammer tokens.
2. Warn everyone that if you use the Planet Hammer to place a Broken World token, everyone will Provoke Outrage of the broken planet's type.
3. Flip this card to its Breaking Worlds side. Keep the 5 Refugees cards tucked under it.
Campaign/11/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Hammer Fragments

Campaign/11/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
When you Provoke Outrage, keep this card and place 2 ships at a planet of the Outrage type.
Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/11/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Sycophants

Campaign/11/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Planet Breaker – Act II.
1. You gain a Flagship—take a Flagship board and set it up as described on the board back.
2. Place 1 fresh starport on your Slipstream Drive Upgrade.
3. Gain Sycophants (02).
4. Gain the Hammer Fragments card (03).
5. Tuck the Planet Hammer (04) and the 5 Refugees cards (05–09) under the Hammer Fragments card.
6. Collect the 6 Hammer Fragment tokens.
7. Roll the event die. Place 1 Hammer Fragment token on each planet of the rolled symbol.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/11/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/12/Fate12.jpg.description:
The merchants have stripped us of our wealth, but riches await those who would seize them.

Your path to power:
II. Fill your Pirate Hoard with stolen resources. Abandon your buildings for a Flagship, but replenish your fleet.
III. Keep your Pirate Haven secret and safe. Rivals can gain Power by finding the Haven.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/12/Fate12.jpg.name: Pirate

Campaign/12/back_0_4.jpg.description:
Pirate – Act III. Laugh at Your Rivals' Folly.
Your Pirate Hoard card has at least 2 resources and...

<target> You choose to declare truthfully that no Rival pieces are in the Pirate Haven's system.
<target> You choose to declare truthfully that a Loyal piece is in the Pirate Haven's system.

At the end of Act III, the Pirate's Rivals in turn order may each choose 1 planet that they control. Reveal the True Rumor tokens to show the Pirate Haven's planet. If any player chose the Pirate Haven's planet, they gain 10 Power and you lose 10 Power. If no one did, you gain 10 Power.
Campaign/12/back_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/12/back_1_4.jpg.description:
Pirate – Act II. <timer> 18–24 (2 players: 24 <timer> / 3 players: 21 <timer> / 4 players: 18 <timer>).

Grow Your Hoard of Resources.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each resource in your Pirate Hoard.
» Basically, you get resources on your Hoard by stealing them.

Embarass Your Rivals with Riches.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 2 for each type of resource that your Pirate Hoard has more of than each Rival.
» Don't count Guild cards.
» With 2 players, count resources on ambition boxes as a Rival.
Campaign/12/back_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/12/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
In Summits, Rivals of the Pirate can take a new Negotiation action, Share Rumor: A Rival places their Loyal agent on a Rumor token that has your Loyal agent.
Now that they have an agent on the Rumor, they can peek at it from now on, and they can take the Share Rumor action to let other Rivals peek at it too.
Campaign/12/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Sharing Rumors

Campaign/12/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
There are 4 Rumors on the map, 2 True and 2 False. The True Rumors saying "Cluster Correct" and "Symbol Correct" indicate the cluster and planet ID of the Haven planet.
At the end of the Act, you gain 10 Power and make the Pirate lose 10 Power if you control the Haven's planet and guess it. (See the Pirate's objective card.)
The Pirate may peek at Rumors. The Pirate's Rivals may peek at Rumors with their Loyal agents.
In battle, if a player steals anything from the Pirate, they may also place 1 agent on a Rumor without a Loyal agent.
Campaign/12/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Rumors of the Haven

Campaign/12/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Crisis: In the rolled cluster, flip all Rumor tokens face up and return all agents on them. If this flipped any Rumors, discard this card.
When Secured: You may place an agent on a Rumor token in a system with Loyal pieces. Discard this card.
Campaign/12/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Rumors Spread 2

Campaign/12/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Crisis: In the rolled cluster, flip all Rumor tokens face up and return all agents on them. If this flipped any Rumors, discard this card.
When Secured: You may place an agent on a Rumor token in a system with Loyal pieces. Discard this card.
Campaign/12/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Rumors Spread 1

Campaign/12/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Pirate – Act III.
1. Shuffle the 2 Rumors Spread cards (09–10) into the Court deck.
2. Place the 2 True Rumor and 2 False Rumor tokens each face down on a different planet. You may peek at them. (Read below for details.)
3. Add Rumors of the Haven (11) and Sharing Rumors (12) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
The True Rumors show the cluster and ID symbol of the Pirate Haven, which you must hide. For example, placing the "Cluster Correct" Rumor on a planet in cluster 5 and the "Symbol Correct" Rumor on a <triangle> planet in cluster 2 means the Haven is secretly at the 5<triangle> planet.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/12/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/12/piece_0_5.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Guild card or resource.
Campaign/12/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Silver-Tongues

Campaign/12/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Crisis: The player with the most resources takes the When Secured action.
When Secured: Draw 1 action card from the bottom of the action discard pile. Discard Call to Action.
Campaign/12/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Call to Action

Campaign/12/piece_1_0.jpg.description: You may tax Rival cities with any Loyal ships, ignoring control, but you do not capture an agent if you do not control it.
Campaign/12/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Pirate Smugglers

Campaign/12/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Pirate – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Pirate Smugglers (05).
2. Scrap Call to Action (06) and Silver-Tongues (07).
3. You may spend Fuel, Material, and Relics on your Pirate Hoard for their Prelude actions, ignoring ship build limits for Material. You may place any resources from it onto your player board. Return all resources remaining on it to the supply.

If you failed your objective:
1. Add your Pirate Fleet (03), Pirate Smugglers (05), Call to Action (06), and Silver-Tongues (07) to the Court deck.
2. Return all resources from your Pirate Hoard to the supply.
Campaign/12/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/12/piece_1_2.jpg.description: When attacking in battle, you may collect raid dice even if there are no defending buildings or Flagships. If you do this, you can only steal resources, max 1 per damaged defending ship.
Campaign/12/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Pirate Fleet

Campaign/12/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Outrage doesn't discard resources here.
You may place resources you steal here. Their raid cost is <key><key> each. (You can spend them and add them to ambitions. Remember, you can raid Free buildings!)
Campaign/12/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Pirate Hoard

Campaign/12/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Pirate – Act II.
1. You gain a Flagship—take a Flagship board and set it up as described on the board back.
2. Place 1 fresh starport each on your Tractor Beam and Defense Array Upgrades.
3. Gain the Pirate Hoard (02) and Pirate Fleet (03).
4. Replenish your fleet—in each cluster where you have no Loyal ships, place 1 ship on the gate.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/12/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/13/Fate13.jpg.description:
The very air of the blighted worlds seems to pulse. The vines grow with purpose and design. We only need to listen.

Your path to power:
II. Spread the Blight and study them while winning ambitions. Replenish your fleet.
III. Dominate the Warlord ambition and ensure the Blight is well-fed. Rivals can gain Power by hunting Blight.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/13/Fate13.jpg.name: Blight Speaker

Campaign/13/back_0_1.jpg.description:
Blight Speaker – Act III. Gorge the Blight with Bodies.

<target> No Rival wins the Warlord ambition.
<target> Blight Hunger has more pieces than the current Chapter.
Campaign/13/back_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/13/back_1_5.jpg.description:
Blight Speaker – Act II. <timer> 14–18 (2 players: 18 <timer> / 3 players: 16 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Contact an Ancient Intelligence.
Each time you win an ambition, advance <timer> 1 for each Blight in systems with Loyal ships.

Spread the Spores of New Life.
Advance <timer> 1 each time you place Blight on the map using your Spore Ships card.
Campaign/13/back_1_5.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/13/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
Place pieces (even yours) destroyed by a Blight Crisis on this card. (They add to one of your grand ambitions.)
After scoring, return all of the pieces from this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Blight Hunger

Campaign/13/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Blight Speaker – Act III.
1. Gain Blight Hunger (13).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/13/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/13/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Crisis: Repair all Blight in the rolled cluster. Place 1 damaged Blight at each planet of the rolled symbol with no Blight. Bury this card.
When Secured: You may destroy all damaged Blight in a cluster with Loyal pieces. Bury this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Blight Looms

Campaign/13/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Crisis: In turn order, each player must return 1 Loyal agent from the Court for each Blight at a Loyal building or Flagship. Bury this card.
When Secured: Destroy all damaged Blight in a cluster with Loyal pieces, taking them as Trophies. Bury this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Blight Panic

Campaign/13/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Crisis: Each player gains 2 Power per Blight Trophy they have. Bury this card.
When Secured: Gain 2 Power per Blight Trophy you have. Return all Blight Trophies of Rivals to the supply. Discard this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Blight Purge 3

Campaign/13/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Crisis: Each player gains 2 Power per Blight Trophy they have. Bury this card.
When Secured: Gain 2 Power per Blight Trophy you have. Return all Blight Trophies of Rivals to the supply. Discard this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Blight Purge 2

Campaign/13/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Crisis: Each player gains 2 Power per Blight Trophy they have. Bury this card.
When Secured: Gain 2 Power per Blight Trophy you have. Return all Blight Trophies of Rivals to the supply. Discard this card.
Campaign/13/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Blight Purge 1

Campaign/13/piece_1_0.jpg.description: Harvest (Tax): Destroy 1 damaged Blight from a system you control. (Do not take it as a Trophy.) Gain 1 resource of the planet in that system, or gain any 1 resource if it is a gate.
Campaign/13/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Blight Reapers

Campaign/13/piece_1_1.jpg.description: When attacking in battle, you can damage and destroy Blight by dealing only 1 hit (instead of 2).
Campaign/13/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Blight Hunters

Campaign/13/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
You can move and repair Blight you control. (They can't Catapult. They're not Loyal.)
Anger (Battle): Roll the number die. For each Psionic you spend, change the roll by 1—"6" wraps to "1" and vice versa. A Blight Crisis happens in each system of the rolled cluster.
Campaign/13/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Blight Fury

Campaign/13/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Blight Speaker – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Blight Fury (04).
2. Add Blight Hunters (05), Blight Reapers (06), and the 3 Blight Purge cards (07–09) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap Blight Panic (10) and Blight Looms (11).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Blight Fury (04), Blight Hunters (05), Blight Reapers (06), and 2 Blight Purge cards (07–08).
2. Add 1 Blight Purge card (09), Blight Panic (10), and Blight Looms (11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/13/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/13/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
When you take a Catapult move, Blight no longer ends it.
Spawn (Build): Damage 1 fresh Blight you control to place 1 damaged Blight in the same system.
Campaign/13/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Spore Ships

Campaign/13/piece_1_5.jpg.description:
Blight Speaker – Act II.
1. Gain Spore Ships (02).
2. Scrap 2 Loyal ships from your supply or the map.
3. Replenish your fleet—if you have fewer than 8 Loyal ships on the map, place 1 ship each in any systems until you have 8 Loyal ships on the map.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/13/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/14/Fate14.jpg.description:
The Reach is dying. To heal our wounds, we must feel each other's pain.

Your path to power:
II. Commit to taking no Trophies or Captives while spreading Psionic energy. Clear all your Outrage and replenish your fleet.
III. Commit to pacifism while ensuring your Rivals have a Psionic connection. Rivals will benefit from your efforts.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/14/Fate14.jpg.name: Pacifist

Campaign/14/back_0_4.jpg.description:
Pacifist – Act III. Demonstrate Nonviolence.

<target> You have no Trophies or Captives.
<target> In total, your Rivals have more Psionic resources than you.
Campaign/14/back_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/14/back_1_4.jpg.description:
Pacifist – Act II. <timer> 12–16 (2 players: 16 <timer> / 3 players: 14 <timer> / 4 players: 12 <timer>).

Commit to Pacifism.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 2 if you have no Trophies, and advance <timer> 1 if you have no Captives.

Spread Empathy.
Advance <timer> 1 for each Psionic you spend in your Prelude while Empath is declared.
Campaign/14/back_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/14/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
If Empath is declared, each player discards all resources matching their Outrage types, and each player with no Psionic Outrage gains 1 Psionic.
Don't discard Guild cards or frozen resources, such as Material and Fuel held in Captives for the Guardian, or resources on Merchant League for the Magnate.
Campaign/14/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Rebuke of the Empaths

Campaign/14/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
In Summits, you can take a new Negotiation action, Forgive: Take a Rival's Outrage as a Favor.
In the Call to Order, the player who called the Summit may Forgive a Rival without their consent by giving a Psionic to that Rival.
The Rival must be able to hold the Psionic. The player who called the Summit may return this Favor now.
If the Psionic Monopoly is in play, the Monopoly holder must give their consent to give the Psionic.
Campaign/14/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Reconcile with the Outraged

Campaign/14/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Tuck this partially under the card play area of the map.
When a player harms anything at a planet that matches a resource they have, they Provoke Outrage of that planet type.
In battle, resolve this at the end of dealing hits and before raiding.
Campaign/14/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Empathy for All 2

Campaign/14/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
When a player harms anything at a planet that matches a resource they have, they Provoke Outrage of that planet type.
For battles, resolve this at the end of dealing hits and before raiding.
Campaign/14/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Empathy for All 1

Campaign/14/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Pacifist – Act III.
1. Add Empathy for All to the rules booklet (09). Explain it to everyone.
2. As a reminder, tuck the second copy of Empathy for All (10) into the card play area of the map.
3. Add Reconcile with the Outraged (11) and Rebuke of the Empaths (12) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/14/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/14/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Crisis: The player with the least Power gains Psionic up to their number of empty resource slots. Discard this card.
When Secured: Gain Psionic up to your number of empty resource slots. Discard this card.
Campaign/14/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Itinerant Empaths 2

Campaign/14/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Crisis: The player with the least Power gains Psionic up to their number of empty resource slots. Discard this card.
When Secured: Gain Psionic up to your number of empty resource slots. Discard this card.
Campaign/14/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Itinerant Empaths 1

Campaign/14/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
When you Copy to influence, you may gain 1 Psionic.
Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Empath ambition.
Campaign/14/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Psionic Liaisons

Campaign/14/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Pacifist – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Scrap your Witnesses card (02).
2. Scrap the Psionic Liaisons (05) and 2 Itinerant Empaths (06–07).
3. Flip any Witnesses on the map to Psionic and add them to the resource supply.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap your Witnesses card (02).
2. Add the Psionic Liaisons (05) and 2 Itinerant Empaths (06–07) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap all Witness tokens on the map.
Campaign/14/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/14/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
When defending in battle, if you take Trophies, return them and gain 1 Psionic for each Trophy returned.
You may hold any number of Psionic here. Their raid cost is <key><key> each.
Campaign/14/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Well of Empathy

Campaign/14/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Witness tokens are your Loyal pieces.
You may move Witnesses you control. When you Catapult move ships, you may move Witnesses along with them.
Prelude: You may flip a Witness you control at a Psionic planet to its Psionic resource side and take it. You may declare Empath.
Campaign/14/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Witnesses

Campaign/14/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Pacifist – Act II.
1. Clear all of your Outrage.
2. Gain the Witnesses (02) and Well of Empathy (03) cards.
3. Collect the 4 Witness tokens. Place 1 Witness on their spyglass side (shown to the right) at each Weapon planet.
4. Replenish your fleet—if you have fewer than 8 Loyal ships on the map, place 1 ship each in any systems until you have 8 Loyal ships on the map.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/14/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/15/Fate15.jpg.description:
For the sake of peace, we must eliminate the tools of suffering.

Your path to power:
II. Control Weapons planets and confiscate Weapons. Clear your Outrage but Provoke Weapon Outrage. Replenish your fleet.
III. Keep the Reach at Peace by enforcing Ceasefires. Rivals can gain Power by controlling systems at Peace.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/15/Fate15.jpg.name: Peacekeeper

Campaign/15/back_0_4.jpg.description:
Peacekeeper – Act III. A Peaceful Reach.

<target> 2 clusters or more are at Peace.
<target> 5 clusters or more are at Peace.
» You can flip clusters to Peace using your Peace Accords card.
» If you're an Outlaw, the First Regent can easily flip clusters to War by using the Empire Balks at Peace edict.
» The Peace Dividends edict can incentivize players to keep clusters at Peace.
Campaign/15/back_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/15/back_1_3.jpg.description:
Peacekeeper – Act II. <timer> 14–18 (2 players: 18 <timer> / 3 players: 16 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Restrict the Weapons Supply.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Weapon you have.

Control the Arms Manufacturers.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Weapons planet you control.
» For objectives, you control Empire-controlled planets if you're the First Regent.
Campaign/15/back_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/15/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
In Summits, you can take a new Negotiation action, Broker Peace: Flip a Ceasefire token from War to Peace, with the consent of players who control at least 2 systems in total in that cluster, including yourself. You may get the consent of Rivals by returning 1 Favor of theirs.
You can return Favors to gain consent from Rivals who control systems even if you didn't call the Summit.
During Summits, the First Regent controls Empire-controlled systems.
Campaign/15/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Peace Accords

Campaign/15/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Players gain 2 Power for each system they control in clusters at Peace.
The First Regent controls Empire-controlled systems but only gains Power for them if the Imperial Policy is Peace.
Campaign/15/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Peace Dividends

Campaign/15/piece_0_2.jpg.description: If the Imperial Policy is War and the Peacekeeper is an Outlaw, the First Regent may flip 1 Ceasefire token to War in a cluster where the Empire controls at least 2 systems.
Campaign/15/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Empire Balks at Peace

Campaign/15/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Each cluster has a Ceasefire token, which shows whether its systems are at War or at Peace. The Peace side, shown to the right, limits harm as follows.
To harm anything in a cluster at Peace except Loyal pieces and Blight, you must spend 1 Weapon. If you do, flip the Ceasefire token in the cluster to War.
Harm means battle, hit, damage, or destroy. It doesn't count Crises.
Ceasefires cannot be removed. They track the states of clusters and are only placed in gates for convenience.
Campaign/15/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Ceasefires

Campaign/15/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Peacekeeper – Act III.
1. Place 1 Ceasefire token near each cluster—we recommend in its gate. Place it on its Peace side (shown right) if you control any systems in that cluster, or on its War side if not. (If you're the First Regent, you control Empire-controlled systems.)
2. With 2 players, return all Weapons from the Warlord ambition box.
3. Add Ceasefires (08), Empire Balks at Peace (09), Peace Dividends (10), and Peace Accords (11) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/15/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/15/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Assemble (Build): Gain 1 Weapon.
Prelude: You may discard this to gain Weapons up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Weapon supply empties, steal the Weapon instead.
Campaign/15/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Arms Interest 2

Campaign/15/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Assemble (Build): Gain 1 Weapon.
Prelude: You may discard this to gain Weapons up to your number of empty resource slots. If the Weapon supply empties, steal the Weapon instead.
Campaign/15/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Arms Interest 1

Campaign/15/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Peacekeeper – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add 2 Arms Interests (05–06) to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap your Oath of Peace (02).
2. Add your Arsenal Keepers (03), if you have it, to the Court deck.
Campaign/15/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/15/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
When you take a Weapon, you may place it here instead. (Even if you're taking for the Imperial Trust!)
Weapons on this card are frozen. Their raid cost is <key><key> each.
Campaign/15/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Arsenal Keepers

Campaign/15/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Scrap this if you're not the Peacekeeper.
Return all Trophies you gain except Blight.
You cannot declare Warlord or gain Power from scoring it. (You can still prevent Rivals from scoring.) You cannot change Imperial Policy to War or enforce Imperial War Policy.
Campaign/15/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Oath of Peace

Campaign/15/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Peacekeeper – Act II.
1. With 2 players, return all Weapons from the Warlord ambition box to the supply.
2. Clear all your Outrage.
3. Provoke Outrage with Weapons. (Discard all Weapons and Weapon Guild cards.)
4. Gain Oath of Peace (02) and Arsenal Keepers (03).
5. Replenish your fleet—if you have fewer than 8 Loyal ships on the map, place 1 ship each in any systems until you have 8 Loyal ships on the map.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/15/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/16/Fate16.jpg.description:
The Reach is too wide for any one empire to hold together. To keep it safe, we must return to the old ways.

Your path to power:
II. Become a Lord by establishing Fiefs and controlling their Seat cities. Replenish your fleet.
III. Keep hold of your Fiefs as feudalism spreads through the Reach. Rivals can gain Power as the Lord of their own Fiefs.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/16/Fate16.jpg.name: Warden

Campaign/16/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Warden – Act III. Return to the Old Ways.

<target> You are Lord of more Fiefs than each Rival.
<target> You control more Seats than the current Chapter.
Campaign/16/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/16/back_2_4.jpg.description:
Warden – Act II. <timer> 22.

Control the Fiefs' Seats.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 2 for each Seat you control.
» Seat tokens are placed using the Claims of Nobility card.

Lord Over the Fiefs.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each city in all Fiefs you are Lord of.
» You are the Lord of a Fief if you placed its Seat.
» When counting cities in your Fiefs, include Free cities and cities with Seat tokens.
Campaign/16/back_2_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/16/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Resolve this for each Fief, starting with Cluster 1 and going clockwise: The Fief's Lord gains 3 Power. Then, if the Fief's Court token is on a card in the Court, the Fief's Lord may influence or secure that card.
Campaign/16/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Lords Gain Power

Campaign/16/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
When you influence an empty card in the Court except the Imperial Council, you may also place the Court token from the supply for a Fief where you are a Lord or Vassal.
To influence or secure a card that has a Court token, you must be a Lord or Vassal in the respective Fief.
If a Lord secures a card with a Court token, they return the Court token and all Vassal agents from the card.
When a player Ransacks the Court, they can secure a card that has a Court token only if they destroyed a city in the respective Fief.
Campaign/16/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Feudal Courts

Campaign/16/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Warden – Act III.
1. Collect the 6 Court tokens in the general supply near the Court. (They are labeled 1st through 6th.)
2. Add Feudal Courts (18) and Lords Gain Power (19) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/16/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/16/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Rivals cannot steal your resources and other Guild cards. (In battle they can steal this first and then spend <key>.)
If this card is stolen, bury it. (Place it on the bottom of the Court deck.)
Campaign/16/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Sworn Guardians

Campaign/16/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
After you roll in battle, you may reroll a number of skirmish dice up to your total Weapon icons from resources and cards.
Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/16/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Skirmishers

Campaign/16/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
While this is in the Court, players cannot harm each other in Fiefs.
Crisis: Scrap this card and agents on it.
When Secured: You may replace 1 Rival city that has a Seat token with a Loyal city. Bury this card.
Campaign/16/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Feast Day 2

Campaign/16/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
While this is in the Court, players cannot harm each other in Fiefs.
Crisis: Scrap this card and agents on it.
When Secured: You may replace 1 Rival city that has a Seat token with a Loyal city. Bury this card.
Campaign/16/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Feast Day 1

Campaign/16/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Levy (Tax): Place 1 ship here from a Vassal's supply. This card can hold a max of 1 ship of a Vassal for each Fief where you are their Lord.
When attacking in battle, ships on this card must take hits first. Return ships here that are destroyed—they are not taken as Trophies.
Campaign/16/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Warden's Levy

Campaign/16/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Warden – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Warden's Levy (12).
2. Add 2 Feast Days (13–14) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap Skirmishers (15) and Sworn Guardians (16).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Claims of Nobility (02) and all Seat tokens that are not on the map.
2. Scrap all Lord title cards (03–08) matching the scrapped Seat tokens.
3. Scrap Warden's Levy (12) and the 2 Feast Days (13–14).
4. Add Skirmishers (15) and Sworn Guardians (16) to the Court deck.
Campaign/16/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/16/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
A Lord may tax any cities in their Fief, ignoring control. (They still take Captives from Rival Vassals' cities!)
If a Lord harms their Vassal in their Fief, or a Vassal harms their Lord in their Fief, they Provoke Outrage with all the Fief's cities. (In battle, resolve this before raiding.)
Vassals do not Provoke Outrage if they destroy a Rival Vassal's city in the Fief.
Feudal Law only triggers from actions that the Lord and their Vassals take within the Fief where they share that relationship, not other Fiefs! In Act III, using the Warden's Levy card does not count as harming your Vassal.
Campaign/16/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Feudal Law

Campaign/16/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Players can add Seat tokens to cities using the Claims of Nobility card.
While a city has a Seat token, its player is the Lord of its cluster, which is the Lord's Fief. They take the Lord title card (03–08) for that cluster.
Rivals of the Lord that have a Loyal city or Flagship in the Lord's Fief are the Lord's Vassals in that Fief alone.
If a city with a Seat is destroyed, return its Seat and matching Lord title card.
The Feudal Law card describes the relationship between Lords and Vassals.
Campaign/16/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Seats and Fiefs

Campaign/16/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 6.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 6 are your Vassals in Cluster 6.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 6, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 6, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 6, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 6.
Campaign/16/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Lord of the 6th Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_1_5.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 5.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 5 are your Vassals in Cluster 5.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 5, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 5, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 5, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 5.
Campaign/16/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Lord of the 5th Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_1_6.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 4.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 4 are your Vassals in Cluster 4.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 4, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 4, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 4, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 4.
Campaign/16/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Lord of the 4th Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_2_0.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 3.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 3 are your Vassals in Cluster 3.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 3, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 3, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 3, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 3.
Campaign/16/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Lord of the 3rd Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_2_1.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 2.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 2 are your Vassals in Cluster 2.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 2, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 2, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 2, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 2.
Campaign/16/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Lord of the 2nd Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_2_2.jpg.description:
Tuck this under your Fate card.
You are the Lord of Cluster 1.
Rivals with a Loyal city or Flagship in Cluster 1 are your Vassals in Cluster 1.
You may tax any cities in Cluster 1, ignoring control. (Take Captives if you tax cities of your Vassals!)
If you harm a Vassal in Cluster 1, or a Vassal harms you in Cluster 1, the harming player Provokes Outrage with all cities in Cluster 1.
Campaign/16/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Lord of the 1st Cluster

Campaign/16/piece_2_3.jpg.description:
Crisis: The player holding this card must take the Claim Fief action.
Claim Fief (Tax): Attach this to a Court card. You may place a Seat token above a Loyal city you control in a cluster with no Seat.
Campaign/16/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Claims of Nobility

Campaign/16/piece_2_4.jpg.description:
Warden – Act II.
1. Collect the 6 Seat tokens. (They are labeled 1st through 6th.)
2. Gain Claims of Nobility (02).
3. Add the 6 Lord title cards (03–08) to the supply.
4. Add the Seats & Fiefs card (09) and Feudal Law card (10) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
5. Replenish your fleet—if you have fewer than 8 Loyal ships on the map, place 1 ship each in any systems until you have 8 Loyal ships on the map.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/16/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/17/Fate17.jpg.description:
I won't bow to you any longer. Tremble before me!

Another path to power:
Control planets and hold resources that match your Outrage. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. When the Edicts are called, your Outrage will make you lose Power as the Reach rejects your rule.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/17/Fate17.jpg.name: Overlord

Campaign/17/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Overlord – Act III. <timer> 20.

Rule by Fear Alone.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each resource type you have that matches your Outrage.

Control by Fear Alone.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 2 for each planet type you control that matches your Outrage.
» If you're First Regent, you control all Empire-controlled systems for objectives.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/17/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/17/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
The Overlord loses Power based on their number of Outrage:
» 1 Outrage: Lose 1 Power
» 2 Outrage: Lose 3 Power
» 3 Outrage: Lose 6 Power
» 4 Outrage: Lose 8 Power
» 5 Outrage: Lose 10 Power
Campaign/17/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Reach Rejects Overlord

Campaign/17/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
When you Provoke Outrage, keep this card, and place 2 ships at a planet of the Outrage type.
Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/17/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Sycophants

Campaign/17/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Overlord – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Sycophants (02).
3. Add Reach Rejects Overlord (03) to the rules booklet.
