4. If you have no Outrage, you may Provoke Outrage once to place 2 ships at a planet of that Outrage type. (Do not also trigger Sycophants.)
Flip this card over.
Campaign/17/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/18/Fate18.jpg.description:
The storm is here. Let me offer you shelter.

Another path to power:
Build Bunkers and take Captives to protect against the Blight. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. You can no longer declare Tyrant, and you gain less Power from winning ambitions.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/18/Fate18.jpg.name: Survivalist

Campaign/18/back_1_0.jpg.description:
Survivalist – Act III. <timer> 14–22 (2 players: 22 <timer> / 3 players: 18 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Keep the People Safe.
Each time you win an ambition, advance <timer> 1 for each Captive you have or for each Bunker on the map, whichever is lower.

Protect Against the Blight.
Advance <timer> 1 each time a Blight Crisis happens at a planet with a Bunker.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/18/back_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/18/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
In turn order, each player with more Power than the Survivalist does this:
If the Survivalist has any Captives that are Loyal to you, you may choose a Bunker that you control.
The Survivalist must either return all of those Captives or Provoke Outrage of that Bunker's planet type.
Campaign/18/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Captive Release

Campaign/18/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
The Survivalist can scrap their agents to build Bunkers on planets, which captures Rival agents and lets them keep Captives when Tyrant is scored.
When a Rival destroys a Bunker, they scrap 1 Captive held by the Survivalist.
Building. Bunkers can be harmed like buildings. (They don't fill building slots and can't be raided.)
Tough. Bunkers take 2 hits each to damage and to destroy.
Campaign/18/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Bunkers

Campaign/18/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Bunker tokens are your Loyal pieces.
You can build Bunkers on planets, max 1 per planet. To build a Bunker, you must also scrap 1 agent from your supply.
When you build a Bunker, capture 1 agent from any Rival's supply. When a Rival destroys a Bunker, they scrap 1 Captive that you have.
Building. Bunkers can be harmed like buildings. (They don't fill building slots and can't be raided.)
Tough. Bunkers take 2 hits each to damage and to destroy.
Campaign/18/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Building Bunkers

Campaign/18/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
You cannot declare the Tyrant ambition.
In scoring, when you win any ambition, you only gain Power for second place. When returning Captives, you keep 1 Captive for each Bunker on the map.
Campaign/18/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Vow of Survival

Campaign/18/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Survivalist – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Vow of Survival (02).
3. Gain the Building Bunkers card (03) and collect the 9 Bunker tokens on it.
4. Add the Bunkers (04) and Captive Release cards (05) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/18/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/19/Fate19.jpg.description:
The Precursors taught us violence. The Keepers perpetuate it. A better future means forgetting the past.

Another path to power:
Collect the Relics and then destroy them. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. Provoke Relic Outrage. You cannot declare Keeper, and you lose Power in the Edicts if Keeper is declared but you're not winning it.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/19/Fate19.jpg.name: Redeemer

Campaign/19/back_0_3.jpg.description:
Redeemer – Act III. <timer> 12–16 (2 players: 16 <timer> / 3 players: 14 <timer> / 4 players: 12 <timer>).

Subdue the Keepers.
After scoring, if you won Keeper or it was not declared, advance <timer> 3.

Shatter the Relics.
After scoring, if you won any ambitions, you may scrap any number of Relics you have. For each you scrap, advance <timer> 2.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/19/back_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/19/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Prelude: Once per turn, you may discard 1 resource to gain 1 Relic.
Campaign/19/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Relic Fence

Campaign/19/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
If Keeper is declared and the Redeemer is not winning it, the Redeemer loses Power equal to Keeper's first place.
If Keeper is not declared, the player with the most Relics may declare Keeper. (The Redeemer can't declare it. On a tie, skip this.)
Campaign/19/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Rebuke of the Keepers

Campaign/19/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
You cannot declare the Keeper ambition.
You can only tax Loyal cities on Relic planets if you control them.
Campaign/19/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Ire of the Keepers

Campaign/19/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Redeemer – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Provoke Relic Outrage.
3. Gain Ire of the Keepers (02).
4. Add Rebuke of the Keepers (03) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
5. Place Relic Fence (04) on top of the Court deck.
6. With 2 players, return all Relics from the Keeper ambition box to the supply.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/19/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/2/Fate2.jpg.description:
Build a new order founded on mutual respect and an open marketplace.

Your path to power:
I. Fulfill your ambitions and control systems as an Outlaw.
II. Lead the Commonwealth and spread Free cities across the map.
III. Protect the Free cities from control by the Empire and players outside the Commonwealth.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/2/Fate2.jpg.name: Founder

Campaign/2/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Founder – Act III. Secure Freedom for All. The Commonwealth won the Commonwealth ambition and...

<target> 3 Free cities are neither controlled by the Empire nor a player who is not a Commonwealth Member.
<target> 7 Free cities are neither controlled by the Empire nor a player who is not a Commonwealth Member.
Campaign/2/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/2/back_2_1.jpg.description:
Founder – Act II. <timer> 12–16 (2 players: 12 <timer> / 3 players: 14 <timer> / 4 players: 16 <timer>).

Distribute Power Fairly. When the Commonwealth wins the Commonwealth ambition, advance <timer> 2 for each Member of the Commonwealth.
» This is explained more on the Commonwealth Ambition card.
» Everyone starts as a Member, but stops being a Member if they harm a Free city.

Protect Freedom in the Reach. At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Free city on the map not controlled by the Empire.
» The Empire controls a system with just 1 fresh Imperial ship.
Campaign/2/back_2_1.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/2/back_3_5.jpg.description:
Founder – Act I. <timer> 14–18 (2 players: 18 <timer> / 3 players: 16 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Inspire Confidence in the Cause. While you're an Outlaw, advance <timer> 3 for each ambition you win.

Defy the Empire's Grip. At the end of a chapter, if you're an Outlaw, advance <timer> 1 for each system you control.
» You can become an Outlaw when you call a Summit or when you declare an ambition, using your Parade Fleets card.
Campaign/2/back_3_5.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/2/piece_0_0.jpg.description: Incite Revolt (Battle): In a system with a fresh Free city, destroy all damaged Rival and Imperial ships, ignoring the Imperial Truce Law. Don't take Trophies.
Campaign/2/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Freedom Fighters

Campaign/2/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Scrap this if there are no Commonwealth Members. The Member of the Commonwealth with the least Power may untuck 1 Armistice card from here and attach it to a card in the Court. On a tie, skip this. Then, the Founder may do the same. The Armistice also prevents Members from leaving the Commonwealth until the Armistice ends!
Campaign/2/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Mass Call for Armistice

Campaign/2/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Founder – Act III.
1. Scrap the Call for Armistice edict (15) and add the Mass Call for Armistice edict (26) to the rules booklet. Explain that it is the same as before, but now the Founder can also call for a Armistice.
2. Gain Freedom Fighters (27).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/2/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/2/piece_0_3.jpg.description: You may secure cards from the Court where you are tied for most agents (but not zero) with a Rival who is not a Commonwealth Member.
Campaign/2/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Spirit of Freedom

Campaign/2/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Founder – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Spirit of Freedom (24).
2. Scrap the 2 Construction Union (08–09) and 2 Admin Union cards (10–11).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Spirit of Freedom (24).
2. Scrap your Founder of the Commonwealth card (13) and the Commonwealth Ambition marker.
3. Add the 2 Construction Union (08–09) and 2 Admin Union cards (10–11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/2/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/2/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Privileges. Members may take actions with Free buildings that are not controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal—tax Free cities, build ships and take Catapult moves with Free starports, build buildings at them, and repair them. Pieces built in this way are always placed fresh.
During Edicts, a Member may stop Members from harming each other and Free cities. (See the Call for Armistice edict card.)
Members can score the Commonwealth ambition together. (See the Commonwealth Ambition law card.)
Disgrace. If a Member harms a Free city, they scrap their Member title. (They cannot become a Member again.)
Campaign/2/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Commonwealth Membership

Campaign/2/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Tuck this partially under your Fate card.
Free Buildings. You may take actions with Free buildings that are not controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal. Pieces built in this way are always placed fresh.
Armistice. During Edicts, a Member may stop Members from harming each other and Free cities. (See the Call for Armistice edict card.)
Commonwealth Ambition. Members can score the Commonwealth ambition together. (See the Commonwealth Ambition law card.)
Disgrace. If you harm a Free city, scrap this title. (You cannot become a Member again!)
Campaign/2/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Member of the Commonwealth 4

Campaign/2/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Tuck this partially under your Fate card.
Free Buildings. You may take actions with Free buildings that are not controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal. Pieces built in this way are always placed fresh.
Armistice. During Edicts, a Member may stop Members from harming each other and Free cities. (See the Call for Armistice edict card.)
Commonwealth Ambition. Members can score the Commonwealth ambition together. (See the Commonwealth Ambition law card.)
Disgrace. If you harm a Free city, scrap this title. (You cannot become a Member again!)
Campaign/2/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Member of the Commonwealth 3

Campaign/2/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Tuck this partially under your Fate card.
Free Buildings. You may take actions with Free buildings that are not controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal. Pieces built in this way are always placed fresh.
Armistice. During Edicts, a Member may stop Members from harming each other and Free cities. (See the Call for Armistice edict card.)
Commonwealth Ambition. Members can score the Commonwealth ambition together. (See the Commonwealth Ambition law card.)
Disgrace. If you harm a Free city, scrap this title. (You cannot become a Member again!)
Campaign/2/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Member of the Commonwealth 2

Campaign/2/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Tuck this partially under your Fate card.
Free Buildings. You may take actions with Free buildings that are not controlled by the Empire like they are Loyal. Pieces built in this way are always placed fresh.
Armistice. During Edicts, a Member may stop Members from harming each other and Free cities. (See the Call for Armistice edict card.)
Commonwealth Ambition. Members can score the Commonwealth ambition together. (See the Commonwealth Ambition law card.)
Disgrace. If you harm a Free city, scrap this title. (You cannot become a Member again!)
Campaign/2/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Member of the Commonwealth 1

Campaign/2/piece_1_3.jpg.description: While this is in the Court, Members of the Commonwealth cannot harm each other or Free buildings. (This card has no Crisis.) When Secured: You must place all agents from here onto other Court cards of your choice. Tuck this card behind Edict 25.
Campaign/2/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Armistice 2

Campaign/2/piece_1_4.jpg.description: While this is in the Court, Members of the Commonwealth cannot harm each other or Free buildings. (This card has no Crisis.) When Secured: You must place all agents from here onto other Court cards of your choice. Tuck this card behind Edict 25.
Campaign/2/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Armistice 1

Campaign/2/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Scrap this if there are no Members of the Commonwealth. The Member of the Commonwealth with the least Power may untuck 1 Armistice card from here and attach it to a card in the Court. On a tie, skip this. The Armistice also prevents Members from leaving the Commonwealth until the Armistice ends!
Campaign/2/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Call for Armistice

Campaign/2/piece_1_6.jpg.description:
The Founder of the Commonwealth can add the Commonwealth Ambition marker to an ambition they declare. Score this ambition as follows:
» This ambition only gives Power for winning it—getting first place with no tie.
» All Members of the Commonwealth combine ambition values into the Commonwealth to determine place for gaining Power. (Use individual ambition values for everything else, like objectives.)
» If the Commonwealth wins, evenly divide the total Power of first and second place among Members. Ignore bonus city Power and Power remainders.
» If a non-Member wins, they gain double the total Power of first and second place.
Campaign/2/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Commonwealth Ambition

Campaign/2/piece_2_0.jpg.description: Tuck this under your Fate card. Keep the Commonwealth Ambition marker on your Fate card. When you declare an ambition, you may also place the Commonwealth Ambition marker from your Fate card onto the declared ambition box. This ambition scores as described on the Commonwealth Ambition law card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Founder of the Commonwealth

Campaign/2/piece_2_1.jpg.description:
Founder – Act II.
1. Gain the Founder of the Commonwealth card (13).
2. Place the Commonwealth Ambition marker on your Fate card.
3. Place the Commonwealth Ambition law (14) and Call for Armistice edict (15) to the rules booklet.
4. Tuck the 2 Armistices (16–17) under Call for Armistice (15).
5. Give a Member of the Commonwealth title card (18–21) to each player, including you.
6. Add Commonwealth Membership (22) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/2/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/2/piece_2_2.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Administration card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Admin Union 2

Campaign/2/piece_2_3.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Administration card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Admin Union 1

Campaign/2/piece_2_4.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Construction card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Construction Union 2

Campaign/2/piece_2_5.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Construction card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Construction Union 1

Campaign/2/piece_2_6.jpg.description: Crisis: Each player gives Favors as follows. If <triangle> was rolled, give one to your left. If <hex> was rolled, give one right. If <moon> was rolled, give one to both your left and right. Bury this card. When Secured: Take 1 Favor from each Rival. Discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Political Intrigue 3

Campaign/2/piece_3_0.jpg.description: Crisis: Each player gives Favors as follows. If <triangle> was rolled, give one to your left. If <hex> was rolled, give one right. If <moon> was rolled, give one to both your left and right. Bury this card. When Secured: Take 1 Favor from each Rival. Discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Political Intrigue 2

Campaign/2/piece_3_1.jpg.description: Crisis: Each player gives Favors as follows. If <triangle> was rolled, give one to your left. If <hex> was rolled, give one right. If <moon> was rolled, give one to both your left and right. Bury this card. When Secured: Take 1 Favor from each Rival. Discard this card.
Campaign/2/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Political Intrigue 1

Campaign/2/piece_3_2.jpg.description: You cannot harm Free buildings. You may build Free buildings, except in Empire-controlled systems. When you build a Free building on a planet, clear your Outrage of its type.
Campaign/2/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Book of Liberation

Campaign/2/piece_3_3.jpg.description:
Founder – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Book of Liberation (04).
2. Add the 3 Political Intrigue cards (05–07) to the Court deck.
3. Place the 2 Construction Union (08–09) and 2 Admin Union cards (10–11) behind the Founder's next Resolution card (22).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap the Book of Liberation (04) and 3 Political Intrigue cards (05–07).
2. Add the 2 Construction Union (08–09) and 2 Admin Union cards (10–11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/2/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/2/piece_3_4.jpg.description: When you declare an ambition, you may place 2 fresh ships at a Free city. Then, if you are a Regent, you may become an Outlaw.
Campaign/2/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Parade Fleets

Campaign/2/piece_3_5.jpg.description:
Founder – Act I.
1. Gain Parade Fleets (02).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/2/piece_3_5.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/20/Fate20.jpg.description:
Our cities are symbols of our shame. If we keep exploiting the bounties of the Reach, nothing will remain.

Another path to power:
Protect the Reach with the new Edenguard ambition—control the most Material and Fuel planets—and by capturing Material and Fuel. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. Provoke Material and Fuel Outrage. You can no longer declare Tycoon or gain Power from it.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/20/Fate20.jpg.name: Guardian

Campaign/20/back_0_3.jpg.description:
Guardian – Act III. <timer> 15.

Give Back to the Land.
At the end of a chapter, if you win Tyrant or it's not declared, advance <timer> 1 for each Material or Fuel in your Captives.

Guard the Reach's Splendor.
At the end of a chapter, if you win Edenguard or it's not declared, advance <timer> 1 for each Material and Fuel planet you control.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/20/back_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/20/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
There is now a new ambition, Edenguard:
Control the most Fuel and Material planets. (For ambitions, the First Regent controls Empire-controlled systems.)
When a player would declare Tycoon, they may declare Edenguard instead.
Campaign/20/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Edenguard Ambition

Campaign/20/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
You cannot declare Tycoon or gain Power from scoring it.
You can only tax Loyal cities on Material and Fuel planets if you control them.
Campaign/20/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Ire of the Tycoons

Campaign/20/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
When you take Material or Fuel, you may place it in your Captives. (Even if you're taking for the Imperial Trust!)
Material and Fuel in your Captives are frozen. (They add to both Tyrant and Tycoon, and they return to the supply if Tyrant is scored.)
Campaign/20/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Green Vault

Campaign/20/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Guardian – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. You Provoke Outrage for Fuel and Material. (Discard these resources and cards. Place agents on their Outrage icons.)
3. With 2 players, return all Material and Fuel on ambition boxes to the supply.
4. Place the Edenguard Ambition tile by the Tycoon ambition box.
5. Gain Green Vault (02) and Ire of the Tycoons (03).
6. Add Edenguard Ambition (04) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/20/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/21/Fate21.jpg.description:
Blight is beautiful. All it needs is a patient gardener.

Another path to power:
Show that Blight is not a threat with the new Blightkin ambition—control the most systems with fresh Blight. You can move and repair Blight, and you can stop Blight Crises in systems with Loyal ships. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. Damaging and destroying Blight now makes you scrap agents.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/21/Fate21.jpg.name: Naturalist

Campaign/21/back_0_3.jpg.description:
Naturalist – Act III. <timer> 14–18 (2 players: 18 <timer> / 3 players: 16 <timer> / 4 players: 14 <timer>).

Live in the Garden.
At the end of a chapter, if you win Blightkin or it is not declared, advance <timer> 1 for each system you control that has any fresh Blight.
» Your Blight Society lets you repair Blight and helps you coexist with them.
» Your Spore Guides helps you add Blight to the map.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/21/back_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/21/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
There is a new ambition, Blightkin: Control the most systems with fresh Blight. (The First Regent controls Empire-controlled systems.)
When a player would declare Empath, they may declare Blightkin instead.
Campaign/21/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Blightkin Ambition

Campaign/21/piece_0_1.jpg.description: Seed (Build): Roll the number die. For each Loyal agent you choose to return from the Court, change the roll by 1—"6" wraps to "1" and vice versa. In the rolled cluster, place 1 damaged Blight in a system with no Blight.
Campaign/21/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Spore Guides

Campaign/21/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
You can repair and move Blight you control. (They can't Catapult. They're not Loyal.)
If you damage or destroy a Blight, scrap 1 Loyal agent from your supply.
In Blight Crises, you may spend 1 Psionic to prevent them in all systems with Loyal ships.
Campaign/21/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Blight Society

Campaign/21/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Naturalist – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Blight Society (02) and Spore Guides (03).
3. Place the Blightkin Ambition tile by the Empath ambition box.
4. Add Blightkin Ambition (04) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/21/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/22/Fate22.jpg.description:
Find the way... Reunite... Make whole...

Another path to power:
Collapse gates to create Passages into the central area of the map, the Twisted Passage, and then control it. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. The Twisted Passage is a space-time anomaly that connects the map in new, strange ways and brings ships back from the dead.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/22/Fate22.jpg.name: Gate Wraith

Campaign/22/back_1_1.jpg.description:
Gate Wraith – Act III. <timer> 18–24 (2 players: 24 <timer> / 3 players: 21 <timer> / 4 players: 18 <timer>).

Collapse the Gates of the Reach.
Each time you win an ambition, advance <timer> 1 for each Passage token on the map.
At the end of a chapter, if you control the Twisted Passage, advance <timer> 1 for each Passage token on the map.
» Check your control of the Twisted Passage before pieces return from The Dead Live.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/22/back_1_1.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/22/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
When Drawn: The player with initiative attaches this to a card in the Court.
Crisis: Hit each piece in the Twisted Passage. Place this under the top card of the Court deck.
When Secured: Move any number of times from the Twisted Passage to any planets. (Don't remove this card!)
Campaign/22/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Passage Storms

Campaign/22/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
The Gate Wraith can use Catapult moves to place Passage tokens on gates.
The map center is now a gate called the Twisted Passage.
Gates with Passage tokens are out of play—pieces that would be placed on them are placed in the Twisted Passage instead.
You may move from systems adjacent to gates with a Passage to the Twisted Passage.
You may move from the Twisted Passage to any planet whose ID symbol is on the event die. After this, reroll the event die.
Players cannot harm Rivals in the Twisted Passage. (Crises can still harm.) Cities in the Twisted Passage are Psionic.
After scoring, The Dead Live places destroyed ships on the Twisted Passage.
Campaign/22/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Passages & The Twisted Passage

Campaign/22/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Any destroyed ships that are not taken as Trophies are placed on this card.
After scoring, take all ships from Trophies and from this card, and place them fresh on the Twisted Passage.
It doesn't matter whether Warlord was scored or not!
Campaign/22/piece_0_2.jpg.name: The Dead Live

Campaign/22/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
When you Catapult move from a planet into the gate of the same cluster, do the following.
1. Place a Passage token on the gate you moved into.
2. Destroy the buildings you used to Catapult—the starport on the map, or your Flagship's Slipstream Drive Upgrade and Armor. Do not take them as Trophies.
3. Place all ships and tokens from the gate onto the Twisted Passage, the central map area. Destroy all buildings on the gate, but do not take them as Trophies.
Campaign/22/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Breaking Gates & Placing Passages

Campaign/22/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
You may Catapult move with Rival starports you control.
When you Catapult move into the Twisted Passage, you may continue your Catapult move, ignoring control and Blight.
Campaign/22/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Catapult Overdrive

Campaign/22/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
Gate Wraith – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Catapult Overdrive (02) and Breaking Gates & Placing Passages (03).
3. Collect the 6 Passage tokens. Place one on any gate. Resolve step 3 of Breaking Gates & Placing Passages (03).
4. Roll the event die and place it in the central map area.
5. Place The Dead Live (04) near the map by the chapter track.
6. Add Passages & The Twisted Passage (05) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
7. Place Passage Storms (06) on top of the Court deck.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/22/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/23/Fate23.jpg.description:
Yes, it was me! I was pulling the strings from the start!

Another path to power:
Hatch Conspiracies that predict who will win ambitions or tie for first place. But don't be too obvious—Rivals can foil you by guessing them. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. You can no longer declare ambitions yourself.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/23/Fate23.jpg.name: Conspirator

Campaign/23/back_1_0.jpg.description:
Conspirator – Act III. <timer> 6–12 (2 players: 12 <timer> / 3 players: 9 <timer> / 4 players: 6 <timer>).

Control the Proceedings.
When scoring ambitions, advance <timer> 1 for each correct Conspiracy and each Conspiracy on unscored ambitions.
» Conspiracies are placed as described on the Conspiracies card and removed by Rivals as described on the Foiling Conspiracies card.
» Conspiracies are scored as described on the Scoring Conspiracies card.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/23/back_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/23/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
The Conspirator has 2 Conspiracy Tokens for each player color.
When you secure any Vox card or a card with Conspirator agents, you may guess a facedown Conspiracy token on an ambition box.
If you are correct, return it to the Conspirator. If you are incorrect, the Conspirator advances <timer> 1.
Campaign/23/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Foiling Conspiracies

Campaign/23/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
There are 2 Conspiracies per player color.
After scoring ambitions, reveal all Conspiracies on scored ambitions.
» The Conspirator advances <timer> 1 for each Conspiracy that is still unrevealed.
» The Conspirator advances <timer> 1 for each revealed Conspiracy showing a player in first place for the ambition or tied for it.
» The Conspirator increases their objective marker on the Power track by <timer> 1 for each Conspiracy that does not show this. (This moves them away from completion.)
Combine all of these steps into a single movement of the objective marker. Return all revealed Conspiracies.
Campaign/23/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Scoring Conspiracies

Campaign/23/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
You have 2 Conspiracy Tokens for each player color.
When you declare an ambition, you do not place an ambition marker. (You cannot declare an ambition if there are no Available markers.) Instead, you place a Conspiracy from your supply facedown in the declared ambition box.
When you secure a card, you may refill the Court with a Guild card you have to place a Conspiracy facedown in an ambition box with no ambition marker.
Campaign/23/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Conspiracies

Campaign/23/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
When you declare an ambition, look at a Rival's hand. You may swap 1 card with them.
Prelude: You may discard this and any number of cards from your hand. Draw the same number of cards (including Farseers) from the bottom of the action discard pile.
Campaign/23/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Farseers

Campaign/23/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Conspirator – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Farseers (02).
3. Collect the 8 Conspiracy tokens—2 per player color. (Even if you don't have all 4 players!)
4. Gain the Conspiracies card (03). Explain it to everyone.
5. Add Scoring Conspiracies (04) and Foiling Conspiracies (05) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/23/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/24/Fate24.jpg.description:
Absolute power corrupts absolutely. Only I can balance the scales.

Another path to power:
Balance the Reach by ensuring players tie for first place in ambitions. You may abandon your Flagship to replenish your fleet. Rivals can now steal your Trophies and Captives. During the Edicts, you must name a Rival as the Judge's Chosen, letting them use your ships.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/24/Fate24.jpg.name: Judge

Campaign/24/back_1_0.jpg.description:
Judge – Act III. <timer> 6.

Balance the Reach.
At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each ambition where any players are tied for first place.
» If you're playing with 2 players, the resources on ambitions can cause a player to tie.
» If a Commonwealth ambition is declared, it only advances the Judge if individual players tie for first place. Do not combine ambition values for this.

At the end of a chapter, you win the game if you completed your objective and have at least 1 Power.
Campaign/24/back_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/24/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
The Judge may declare any ambition, placing any unplaced ambition marker. (It doesn't need to have the most Power.) If they do, they lose Power for second place on the declared ambition marker.
Then, return the Judge's Chosen card to the Judge. The Judge must give it to any Rival.
Campaign/24/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Judge Sets Agenda

Campaign/24/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
If you're a Rival of the Judge, follow these rules:
You may take actions with the Judge's Loyal ships. (Their ships don't add to your control.)
When attacking anyone except the Judge in battle, you may add the Judge's Loyal ships in the battle system to your attacking ships.
When attacking the Judge in battle, Judge's Loyal ships add to their defending pieces, not your attacking ships.
Campaign/24/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Judge's Chosen

Campaign/24/piece_0_2.jpg.description: At the start of scoring, you may give any number of your Trophies, Captives, and resources to Rivals, up to their number of empty resource slots. Pieces Loyal to that Rival return to their supply.
Campaign/24/piece_0_2.jpg.name: The Arbiter

Campaign/24/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Your Trophies and Captives can be stolen. Their raid cost is <key> each.
If a player steals Trophies or Captives that are Loyal to them, those pieces return to their supply.
Campaign/24/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Vow of Fairness

Campaign/24/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Judge – Act III.
1. If you have a Flagship, you may Resettle—as described on the back of the Flagship board.
2. Gain Vow of Fairness (02), The Arbiter (03), and Judge's Chosen (04).
3. Add Judge Sets Agenda (05) to the rules booklet.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/24/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/3/Fate3.jpg.description:
Corner the market and dominate the Reach with your commercial prowess.

Your path to power:
I. Barter for resources and Favors from Rivals during Summits.
II. Take and hold Monopolies over the resource supplies.
III. Hold Monopolies and escape the Reach with your riches.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/3/Fate3.jpg.name: Magnate

Campaign/3/back_0_1.jpg.description: While the Magnate is a Outlaw, players may tax Loyal cities more than once per turn. The Magnate may also tax Rival and Free cities more than once per turn. If the Magnate becomes a Regent, flip this to Closed Economy.
Campaign/3/back_0_1.jpg.name: Open Economy

Campaign/3/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act III. Escape the Reach, Content with Your Riches.

<target> Have any Monopolies.
<target> Have at least 2 Monopolies.

After scoring, you must scrap 1 Monopoly you have and all of your resources of that Monopoly type.
» Scrap all resources in your play area, whether they're on your player board, on the Monopoly, on Merchant League, or otherwise!
» If you have no Monopolies, do not scrap resources.
Campaign/3/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/3/back_2_1.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act II. <timer> 12.

Corner the Market... Advance <timer> 1 each time you gain a Monopoly card from the supply.
» Players can gain Monopolies as described on the Gain Monopolies edict in the rules booklet.

...And Hold It. At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Monopoly card you have.
Campaign/3/back_2_1.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/3/back_3_5.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act I. <timer> 10.

Close Deals with Rivals. In Summit Negotiations, advance <timer> 2 each time a Rival gives you a Favor or resource, except one that you gave them during this Summit.

Export to the Galactic Core. In Summit Negotiations, advance <timer> 2 each time you choose to discard 3 resources.
» You can fulfill both objectives multiple times in one Summit!
» Summits happen from Event cards and the Imperial Council.
» You can't discard resources from your Merchant League—they're frozen except for the Transfer Asset action.
Campaign/3/back_3_5.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/3/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
The Economy of the Reach is shown by the Economy card (26) tucked under the card play area on the map.

While the Magnate is a Regent, the Empire has a Closed Economy: Regents cannot tax Empire-controlled cities (even Loyal ones) unless they have the matching Monopoly.

While the Magnate is an Outlaw, the Reach has an Open Economy: Players may tax Loyal cities more than once per turn. The Magnate may also tax Rival and Free cities more than once per turn.
Campaign/3/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Closed & Open Economy

Campaign/3/piece_0_1.jpg.description: While the Magnate is a Regent, Regents other than the Magnate cannot tax Empire-controlled cities (even Loyal ones) unless they have the matching Monopoly. If the Magnate becomes an Outlaw, flip this to Open Economy.
Campaign/3/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Closed Economy

Campaign/3/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act III.
1. If you're a Regent, tuck the Economy law card (26) on its Closed Economy side under the card play area near the map. If you're an Outlaw, tuck it on its Open Economy side.
2. Add Closed & Open Economy (27) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
3. Remember to place the War Profiteer marker on the Warlord ambition box.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/3/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/3/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Crisis: For each resource type, the player with most of it, or the players tied for the most of it, must discard 1 resource of that type. Discard this card. When Secured: Steal 1 resource from a Rival. Discard this card.
Campaign/3/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Extortion 2

Campaign/3/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Crisis: For each resource type, the player with most of it, or the players tied for the most of it, must discard 1 resource of that type. Discard this card. When Secured: Steal 1 resource from a Rival. Discard this card.
Campaign/3/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Extortion 1

Campaign/3/piece_0_5.jpg.description: You may build cities on any planets if you don't have the Monopoly matching that planet type. (You don't need Loyal pieces there!)
Campaign/3/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Prospectors

Campaign/3/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Prospectors (22).
2. Scrap the 5 Cartel cards (07–11) and the 2 Extortion cards (23–24).
3. If Mining Interest (CC01) or Fuel Interest (CC04) are in the Court discard pile, add them to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Prospectors (22) and any Monopoly cards (13–17) that players do not have.
2. Add the Cartel cards (07–11) matching the scrapped Monopolies to the Court deck. Scrap the rest of the Cartel cards (07–11).
3. Add the 2 Extortion cards (23–24) to the Court deck.
Campaign/3/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/3/piece_1_0.jpg.description: In Act II & III setup, place the War Profiteer marker on the Warlord ambition box. The Warlord ambition now counts the total of Trophies and Weapons icons from resources and cards.
Campaign/3/piece_1_0.jpg.name: War Profiteering

Campaign/3/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
In Summits, players may only give a resource with Transfer Asset if they have the consent of the player with the Monopoly for that resource. Consent is not needed if no one has the Monopoly.

In the Call to Order, the player who called the Summit may force the Monopoly holder's consent by returning 1 Favor of theirs to them.
Campaign/3/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Monopoly Consent

Campaign/3/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
For each resource type, a player takes the Monopoly card of its type if they have more than half of the total resources of its type in play.

Usually this means you need 3 resources out of 5. Some Fates add resources or scrap them, removing them from play. Two-player games remove resources from play onto ambition boxes.

Monopolies let you hold the matching resource supply. You add resources on Monopolies to ambitions, but you can't spend them. Also, players need your consent to give that resource during Summits.
Campaign/3/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Gain Monopolies

Campaign/3/piece_1_3.jpg.description: You hold the Weapon supply here. (Add Weapons on here to Warlord. You cannot spend them.) In Summits, your consent is required to give Weapons.
Campaign/3/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Weapon Monopoly

Campaign/3/piece_1_4.jpg.description: You hold the Psionic supply here. (Add Psionic on here to Empath. You cannot spend it.) In Summits, your consent is required to give Psionic.
Campaign/3/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Psionic Monopoly

Campaign/3/piece_1_5.jpg.description: You hold the Relic supply here. (Add Relics on here to Keeper. You cannot spend them.) In Summits, your consent is required to give Relics.
Campaign/3/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Relic Monopoly

Campaign/3/piece_1_6.jpg.description: You hold the Fuel supply here. (Add Fuel on here to Tycoon. You cannot spend it.) In Summits, your consent is required to give Fuel.
Campaign/3/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Fuel Monopoly

Campaign/3/piece_2_0.jpg.description: You hold the Material supply here. (Add Material on here to Tycoon. You cannot spend it.) In Summits, your consent is required to give Material.
Campaign/3/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Material Monopoly

Campaign/3/piece_2_1.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act II.
1. Place the 5 Monopoly cards (13–17) in the general supply. Place the resource supplies on their respective Monopoly cards.
2. Add the Gain Monopolies edict (18) to the rules booklet.
3. Place the War Profiteer marker on the Warlord ambition box.
4. Add the Monopoly Consent card (19) and War Profiteering card (20) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/3/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/3/piece_2_2.jpg.description: You keep the Relic supply on here. (You add it to Keeper but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Relics. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Relic.
Campaign/3/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Relic Cartel

Campaign/3/piece_2_3.jpg.description: You keep the Psionic supply on here. (You add it to Empath but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Psionic. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Psionic.
Campaign/3/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Psionic Cartel

Campaign/3/piece_2_4.jpg.description: You keep the Weapon supply on here. (You add it to Warlord but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Weapons. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Weapon.
Campaign/3/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Weapon Cartel

Campaign/3/piece_2_5.jpg.description: You keep the Fuel supply on here. (You add it to Tycoon but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Fuel. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Fuel.
Campaign/3/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Fuel Cartel

Campaign/3/piece_2_6.jpg.description: You keep the Material supply on here. (You add it to Tycoon but can't spend it.) After scoring, Rivals discard all Material. Prelude: You may discard this to steal 1 Material.
Campaign/3/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Material Cartel

Campaign/3/piece_3_0.jpg.description: Pressgang (Build): Return any number of your Captives to gain any 1 resource for each. Execute (Influence): Move any number of your Captives to your Trophies. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/3/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Prison Wardens

Campaign/3/piece_3_1.jpg.description: Prelude: Once per turn, you may discard 1 resource to gain 1 Relic.
Campaign/3/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Relic Fence

Campaign/3/piece_3_2.jpg.description: Trade (Tax): Choose a Rival city you control. Swap 1 resource with that Rival—take a resource of that city type from them, and give them a resource they don't have. Prelude: You may discard this to gain 1 Material, 1 Fuel, and 1 Weapon.
Campaign/3/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Elder Broker

Campaign/3/piece_3_3.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add the Elder Broker (04), Relic Fence (05), and Prison Wardens (06) to the Court deck.
2. Place the 5 Cartel cards (07–11) behind the Magnate's next Resolution card (21).
3. If Mining Interest (CC01) or Shipping Interest (CC04) are in the Court discard pile, add them to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap the Elder Broker (04), Relic Fence (05), and Prison Wardens (06).
2. Add the 5 Cartel cards (07–11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/3/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/3/piece_3_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Discard all resources from here. When you declare an ambition, you may gain any 2 resources onto here. These resources are frozen except for the Transfer Asset action in Summits.
Campaign/3/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Merchant League

Campaign/3/piece_3_5.jpg.description:
Magnate – Act I.
1. Gain Merchant League (02).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/3/piece_3_5.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/4/Fate4.jpg.description:
Connect with the ancient guilds of the Reach and make yourself indispensable.

Your path to power:
I. Use and recover as many Guild cards as you can while proving yourself to the Guild Overseers.
II. Have more Guild cards than your Rivals in a variety of suits.
III. Fill the Court with Guild cards and keep a variety of Guild suits.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/4/Fate4.jpg.name: Advocate

Campaign/4/back_0_3.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act III. Consolidate Guild Supremacy.

<target> Have more different suits of Guild cards than the current Chapter.
<target> Guild cards make up more than half of the Court.
» The second grand ambition counts attached cards. For example, if the Court has the Imperial Council, 3 cards, and 2 attached cards, this grand ambition requires 4 Guild cards.
» Remember that you can attach a Guild card to the Imperial Council, since it's a card in the Court. It doesn't even have to be In Session!
Campaign/4/back_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/4/back_2_0.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act II. <timer> 12.

Connect with the Guilds. At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each different suit of Guild card you have.

Improve Your Standing. At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Rival that you have more Guild cards than.
» The Advocate's Demand edict will help you advance these objectives!
Campaign/4/back_2_0.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/4/back_3_4.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act I. <timer> 18.

Prove Yourself to the Overseers. While you have Guild Overseers, advance <timer> 3 each time you use the Prelude action of a Guild card that discards itself.
» The Guild Overseers card limits when you can use Guild cards, including Guild Investigators!

Keep the Guilds in Power. While you have Guild Overseers, advance <timer> 2 each time you take the Recover action of your Guild Investigators.
Campaign/4/back_3_4.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/4/piece_0_0.jpg.description: When scoring, Guild cards count twice. For example, each Psionic Guild card counts as two toward the Empath ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Guild Supremacy 2

Campaign/4/piece_0_1.jpg.description: When scoring, Guild cards count twice. For example, each Psionic Guild card counts as two toward the Empath ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Guild Supremacy 1

Campaign/4/piece_0_2.jpg.description: When you Provoke Outrage, you do not bury Guild cards.
Campaign/4/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Guild Loyalty

Campaign/4/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act III.
1. Gain Guild Loyalty (24).
2. Add Guild Supremacy to the rules booklet (25). Explain it to everyone.
3. As a reminder, place the second copy of Guild Supremacy (26) near the ambition boxes.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/4/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/4/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Crisis: If a <hex> or <triangle> was rolled, each player in turn order must bury 1 Guild card. Bury this card. When Secured: Each Rival must bury 1 Guild card. Bury this card.
Campaign/4/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Guilds Withdraw

Campaign/4/piece_0_5.jpg.description: When you declare the Empath ambition, you may steal a Psionic Guild card.
Campaign/4/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Psionic Guildmasters

Campaign/4/piece_0_6.jpg.description: When you declare the Keeper ambition, you may steal a Relic Guild card.
Campaign/4/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Relic Guildmasters

Campaign/4/piece_1_0.jpg.description: When you declare the Warlord ambition, you may steal a Weapon Guild card.
Campaign/4/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Weapons Guildmasters

Campaign/4/piece_1_1.jpg.description: When you declare the Tycoon ambition, you may steal a Fuel Guild card.
Campaign/4/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Fuel Guildmasters

Campaign/4/piece_1_2.jpg.description: When you declare the Tycoon ambition, you may steal a Material Guild card.
Campaign/4/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Material Guildmasters

Campaign/4/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Crisis: Players secure all Guild cards that they currently can. Bury this card. When Secured: Return all Rival agents from Guild cards in the Court. Secure all Guild cards that you currently can. Bury this card.
Campaign/4/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Guild Fair

Campaign/4/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add Guild Fair (16) and the 5 Guildmaster cards (17–21) to the Court deck.
2. Scrap Guilds Withdraw (22).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Guild Fair (16) and the 5 Guildmaster cards (17–21).
2. Add Guilds Withdraw (22) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap Advocate's Demand (13) from the rules booklet.
Campaign/4/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/4/piece_1_5.jpg.description: In Summits, you can take a new Negotiation action, Transfer Guild Support: Give a Guild card. This requires the Advocate's consent if they are in play. Forcing this with Return Favors costs Favors equal to the Guild card's raid cost. You may force the Advocate's consent by returning 1 Favor of theirs to them. Players cannot be forced to give protected Guild cards.
Campaign/4/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Guild Negotiations

Campaign/4/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Scrap this if the Advocate is not in play. The Advocate may choose 1 Guild card held by a Rival, but its suit cannot match any Guild card held by the Advocate. That Rival must either give that Guild card to the Advocate and take 1 Favor from the Advocate, or give 1 Favor to the Advocate. This is not stealing, so cards like Sworn Guardians don't prevent it. The Advocate can even demand protected Guild cards!
Campaign/4/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Advocate's Demand

Campaign/4/piece_2_0.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act II.
1. Add the Advocate's Demand edict (13) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
2. Add Guild Negotiations (14) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/4/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/4/piece_2_1.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player gives 1 Guild card to their left, except the card they were given. Bury Guild Struggle. When Secured: You may steal 1 Guild card from a Rival. Shuffle all Guild cards from the Court discard pile into the Court deck. Bury Guild Struggle.
Campaign/4/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Guild Struggle

Campaign/4/piece_2_2.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player with more Guild cards than agents in their supply must discard Guild cards until their Guild cards equal their agents. Bury this card. (Count Protected Guild cards but don't discard them.) When Secured: Rivals resolve the Crisis.
Campaign/4/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Diplomatic Fiasco

Campaign/4/piece_2_3.jpg.description: When you Copy to influence, you may gain 1 Psionic. Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Empath ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Psionic Liaisons

Campaign/4/piece_2_4.jpg.description: When you Copy to secure, you may gain 1 Relic. Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Keeper ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Relic Liaisons

Campaign/4/piece_2_5.jpg.description: When you Copy to battle, you may gain 1 Weapon. Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Warlord ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Weapons Liaisons

Campaign/4/piece_2_6.jpg.description: When you Copy to move, you may gain 1 Fuel. Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Tycoon ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Fuel Liaisons

Campaign/4/piece_3_0.jpg.description: When you Copy to build, you may gain 1 Material. Prelude: You may attach this to a Court card with no agents to declare the Tycoon ambition.
Campaign/4/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Material Liaisons

Campaign/4/piece_3_1.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add your Guild Overseers (03), the 5 Liaison cards (05–09), and Diplomatic Fiasco (10) to the Court deck.
2. Scrap Guild Struggle (11).

If you failed your objective:
1. Add your Guild Overseers (03) to the Court deck.
2. For each Guild card suit that you have more of than each Rival, add the matching Liaison card (05–09) to the Court deck. Scrap the remaining Liaison cards.
3. Scrap Diplomatic Fiasco (10).
4. Add Guild Struggle (11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/4/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/4/piece_3_2.jpg.description: To use actions on Guild cards, you must be in first or second place for a declared ambition. (That is, you'd gain Power if it scored now.) When Secured: Search the Court deck and discard pile to take any 1 Guild card. If you searched the deck, shuffle it.
Campaign/4/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Guild Overseers

Campaign/4/piece_3_3.jpg.description: Recover (Move): Attach the topmost Guild card from the Court discard pile to a card in the Court with no agents.
Campaign/4/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Guild Investigators

Campaign/4/piece_3_4.jpg.description:
Advocate – Act I.
1. Gain Guild Investigators (02) and Guild Overseers (03). Guild Overseers has a When Secured action. Do not resolve this now. It is only relevant once Guild Overseers enters the Court deck in Act II.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/4/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/5/Fate5.jpg.description:
Awaken the Golems to safeguard the wisdom of the Precursors.

Your path to power:
I. Control and awaken the Sleeping Golems, then use them.
II. Collect lore from the Court and from your Rivals in Summits.
III. Use your Golems to scrap your lore and the Golems themselves.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/5/Fate5.jpg.name: Caretaker

Campaign/5/back_0_1.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act III. Consume Dangerous Lore with the Golems, but Silence Them Before They Eat Everything.

<target> At the end of a chapter, you have a Golem and choose to scrap 1 lore card you have except the Golem Hearth.
<target> At the end of a chapter, you choose to scrap 1 Golem you have.
» You can take each of these actions once per chapter. You may scrap the Golem after scrapping the lore card.
Campaign/5/back_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/5/back_1_2.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act II. <timer> 7–9 (2 players: 7 <timer> / 3 players: 8 <timer> / 4 players: 9 <timer>).

Collect the Galaxy's Knowledge. Advance <timer> 2 each time you secure a lore card. In Summit Negotiations, advance <timer> 1 each time a Rival gives you a lore card using Transfer Lore, unless you gave them that lore card during the same Summit.
» The Knowledge Set Free card lets players give away lore in Summits.

Build the Largest Collection. At the end of a chapter, advance <timer> 1 for each Rival that you have more lore cards than.
Campaign/5/back_1_2.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/5/back_2_6.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act I. <timer> 18.

Find the Golems. Each time you win an ambition, advance <timer> 2 for each Golem you have.
» You can take Golems from the map with your Golem Beacon card.

Use the Golems. Advance <timer> 2 each time any player spends a Golem in their Prelude.
» Advance even if the player chooses not to use the Golem's Prelude action.
Campaign/5/back_2_6.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/5/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
First, Consume Sentience. In turn order, each player must scrap 1 Guild card for each Awake Golem they have, ignoring the limits of protected Guild cards. (In other words, you can scrap protected Guild cards too!) If they have no Guild cards, scrap the leftmost Guild card in the Court that has any agents, and scrap those agents.

Then, Golems Awaken. Flip all Sleeping Golems to Awake.
Campaign/5/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Golems Sate Their Appetite

Campaign/5/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act III.
1. Add the Golems Sate Their Appetite edict (21) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
2. Flip all Sleeping Golems to Awake.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/5/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/5/piece_0_2.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player with any Golems must return 1 Loyal city to their player board. Bury this card. When Secured: Take any Golem and flip it to Awake. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Golem Worship 3

Campaign/5/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player with any Golems must return 1 Loyal city to their player board. Bury this card. When Secured: Take any Golem and flip it to Awake. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Golem Worship 2

Campaign/5/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Crisis: In turn order, each player with any Golems must return 1 Loyal city to their player board. Bury this card. When Secured: Take any Golem and flip it to Awake. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Golem Worship 1

Campaign/5/piece_0_5.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add the 3 Golem Worship cards (17–19) to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap the 3 Golem Worship cards (17–19).
2. Add all Unstable cards (08–11) from the Court discard pile to the Court deck.
Campaign/5/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/5/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Mirror Lead, Add Event to Round End. Take actions like the lead card unless a Rival played an Event card or secured the Imperial Council this round. You cannot Lead this, Copy with this, or discard this to seize the initiative.

At the end of the round, after checking initiative, the player with initiative may call a Summit, then must roll an event. <crisis>: Resolve Crises. <edict>: Resolve Edicts. If the Imperial Council was secured this round, resolve it instead of this.
Campaign/5/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Event

Campaign/5/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
In Summits, you can take these new Negotiation actions:
Transfer Lore: Give a lore card to a Rival. This cannot be forced with Return Favors.
Transfer Golem: Give a Golem to a Rival. To force this with Return Favors, you must return Favors equal in number to the Golem's raid cost.
Campaign/5/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Knowledge Set Free

Campaign/5/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Crisis: The player holding this card must attach it to a card in the Court. After you secure a card, you may draw 1 lore card from the lore deck and attach it to the card that refilled the Court.
Campaign/5/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Librarians

Campaign/5/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act II.
1. Gain Librarians (13).
2. Add Knowledge Set Free (14) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
3. Shuffle the extra Event card (15) into the action deck. Mention this to everyone. (This card does not list its card number.)
4. Flip all Sleeping Golems to Awake.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/5/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/5/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Crisis: Destroy all damaged Loyal ships of the player with the Warrior. When Secured: Flip the Warrior to Awake and take it. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Unstable Warrior

Campaign/5/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Repair all ships except those of the player with the Protector. When Secured: Flip the Protector to Awake and take it. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Unstable Protector

Campaign/5/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Crisis: The player with the Seeker moves their Loyal ships from planets of the rolled symbol to the gate in the rolled cluster. When Secured: Flip the Seeker to Awake and take it. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Unstable Seeker

Campaign/5/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Crisis: The player with the Harvester discards all of their resources. When Secured: Flip the Harvester to Awake and take it. Discard this card.
Campaign/5/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Unstable Harvester

Campaign/5/piece_2_0.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Scrap your Golem Beacon (02).
2. Place all Golem tokens from the map onto your Golem Hearth (03).
3. Add 1 lore card per player at random from the lore deck to the Court deck. Add the 4 Unstable cards (08–11) to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap your Golem Beacon (02).
2. Add your Stone-Speakers (04) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap all Golem tokens on the map.
4. Add the Unstable cards (08–11) matching the Golems still in play to the Court deck. Scrap the rest of the Unstable cards (08–11).
Campaign/5/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/5/piece_2_1.jpg.description:
Prelude: You may spend your Awake Golems to optionally take their actions.
Warrior: Destroy all damaged ships in one system. (Return Loyal ships to your supply.)
Protector: Repair any ships of your choice. (Also 1 Upgrade or Armor with a Flagship.)
Seeker: Take a Catapult move from one system.
Harvester: Gain any resource.
Campaign/5/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Golem Actions

Campaign/5/piece_2_2.jpg.description:
Only the Caretaker can take Golems from the map. Golems fill resource slots and can be discarded, rearranged, and spent for actions like resources. (See the Golem Actions card for details.)

They have Sleeping and Awake sides. Its Awake side is public if a player holds it. (Players' Golems flip to Awake in Crises.)

When a Golem is spent or discarded, flip it to Sleeping and place it in a Rival's empty resource slot. If there are none, you keep it.

In Act II/III setup, Golems flip to Awake.
Campaign/5/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Golems

Campaign/5/piece_2_3.jpg.description: When you declare an ambition, you may end your turn immediately. If you do, take any Golems from Rivals, then roll the event die and resolve Crises if you roll it. (Don't resolve Edicts.)
Campaign/5/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Stone-Speakers

Campaign/5/piece_2_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Flip all Sleeping Golems that are not on the map to Awake. This card holds any number of Golems. (You may spend Awake Golems here.) Their raid cost is <key><key> each.
Campaign/5/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Golem Hearth

Campaign/5/piece_2_5.jpg.description: Awaken (Influence): Take any Golems you control from the map and place them on your Golem Hearth card. Flip these Golems to Awake if you are winning a declared ambition.
Campaign/5/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Golem Beacon

Campaign/5/piece_2_6.jpg.description:
Caretaker – Act I.
1. Gain Golem Beacon (02), Golem Hearth (03), and Stone-Speakers (04).
2. Shuffle the 4 Sleeping Golem tokens. Place one on its Sleeping side at each gate in the clusters without Imperial ships.
3. Add Golems (05) and Golem Actions (06) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/5/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/6/Fate6.jpg.description:
Destroy the oppressors of the Reach by any means necessary.

Your path to power:
I. Get initiative even if you Provoke Outrage, and fulfill the ambitions favored by your supporters.
II. Hold initiative and gather Guild support to burnish your image.
III. Have the most Guild cards and continue to hold the initiative.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/6/Fate6.jpg.name: Partisan

Campaign/6/back_0_1.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act III. Form a Dominant Power Bloc.

<target> Have more Guild cards than each Rival.
<target> Have the initiative marker.
Campaign/6/back_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/6/back_1_3.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act II. <timer> 12–18 (2 players: 18 <timer> / 3 players: 14 <timer> / 4 players: 12 <timer>).

Show Your Leadership. Advance <timer> 1 each time you play an action card while you have the initiative.

Burnish Your Image. When you return Outrage using People's Hero, advance <timer> 1 for each Guild card you have.
Campaign/6/back_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/6/back_3_3.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act I. <timer> 10–12 (2 players: 12 <timer> / 3 players: 11 <timer> / 4 players: 10 <timer>).

Sow Division in the Reach. Advance <timer> 1 each time you get the initiative marker in any way.
» Your Partisan Seizing card changes how you seize the initiative—instead of discarding an extra card, you need to Provoke Outrage.

Appeal to Your Supporters. Advance <timer> 2 each time you win an ambition where you don't have Outrage for the resource it counts. Weapon Outrage stops you from advancing by winning Warlord.
Campaign/6/back_3_3.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/6/piece_0_0.jpg.description: At the end of a round, when you get the initiative marker from Surpassing, you may place a Loyal agent on a Rival's empty Outrage slot. (This does not Provoke Outrage.)
Campaign/6/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Sower of Division

Campaign/6/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act III.
1. Gain Sower of Division (25).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/6/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/6/piece_0_2.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Aggression card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Arms Union 2

Campaign/6/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Mobilization card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Spacing Union 2

Campaign/6/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Destroy all damaged pieces at planets matching any players' Outrage. Shuffle this into the Court deck. When Secured: Resolve the Crisis but do not destroy Loyal pieces.
Campaign/6/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Terror Strike 2

Campaign/6/piece_0_5.jpg.description: Crisis: Destroy all damaged pieces at planets matching any players' Outrage. Shuffle this into the Court deck. When Secured: Resolve the Crisis but do not destroy Loyal pieces.
Campaign/6/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Terror Strike 1

Campaign/6/piece_0_6.jpg.description: If Diplomatic Fiasco is not in the Court, search the Court deck for it, attach it to the Imperial Council card, and flip the Imperial Council to its In Session side.
Campaign/6/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Guilds Decry Terror

Campaign/6/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add Guilds Decry Terror (19) to the rules booklet.
2. Add 2 Terror Strikes (20–21) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap the Spacing Union (22) and Arms Union (23).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Guilds Decry Terror (19) and the 2 Terror Strikes (20–21).
2. Add Spacing Union (22) and Arms Union (23) to the Court deck.
Campaign/6/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/6/piece_1_1.jpg.description: When you declare an ambition, you may clear 1 Outrage you have as long as you have more Guild cards of the Outrage type than each Rival.
Campaign/6/piece_1_1.jpg.name: People's Hero

Campaign/6/piece_1_2.jpg.description: You cannot seize the initiative by discarding a second action card. (This doesn't limit other ways to seize.) Instead, you may seize the initiative when playing a card if you choose to Provoke Outrage with a non-Outraged resource. (Discard resources and Guild cards of the Outrage type. Place an agent on the matching Outrage slot.) Remember, you can't seize the initiative if you have it or it has been seized!
Campaign/6/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Partisan Seizing 2

Campaign/6/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act II.
1. Add Partisan Seizing (16) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
2. As a reminder, tuck your copy of Partisan Seizing (04) under the map near the card play area, so only its title shows.
3. Gain People's Hero (17).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/6/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/6/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Each player Provokes Outrage of the rolled planet. Discard this card. When Secured: You may choose an Outrage type. Each player (even you) must Provoke Outrage of that type. Discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Outrage Spreads 2

Campaign/6/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Crisis: Each player Provokes Outrage of the rolled planet. Discard this card. When Secured: You may choose an Outrage type. Each player (even you) must Provoke Outrage of that type. Discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Outrage Spreads 1

Campaign/6/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Aggression card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Arms Union 1

Campaign/6/piece_2_0.jpg.description: Prelude: You may place this card next to a face-up played Mobilization card. When the round ends, draw that card into your hand and discard this card.
Campaign/6/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Spacing Union 1

Campaign/6/piece_2_1.jpg.description: When you Surpass or Pivot, before taking any actions, you may declare the ambition on your played card if an ambition has not been declared yet this round. Do not place the zero marker.
Campaign/6/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Galactic Bards

Campaign/6/piece_2_2.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Relics. You ignore Outrage when spending Relics for their Prelude action.
Campaign/6/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Loyal Keepers

Campaign/6/piece_2_3.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Psionic. You ignore Outrage when spending Psionic for its Prelude action.
Campaign/6/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Loyal Empaths

Campaign/6/piece_2_4.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Weapons. You ignore Outrage when spending Weapons for their Prelude action. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/6/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Loyal Marines

Campaign/6/piece_2_5.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Fuel. You ignore Outrage when spending Fuel for its Prelude action.
Campaign/6/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Loyal Pilots

Campaign/6/piece_2_6.jpg.description: If you Provoke Outrage, keep this card. You may spend any resources as Material. You ignore Outrage when spending Material for its Prelude action.
Campaign/6/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Loyal Engineers

Campaign/6/piece_3_0.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add the 5 Loyal cards (05–09) to the Court deck.
2. Gain Galactic Bards (10).
3. Scrap the Spacing Union (11), Arms Union (12), and 2 Outrage Spreads (13–14).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap the 5 Loyal cards (05–09).
2. Add Galactic Bards (10), Spacing Union (11), Arms Union (12), and 2 Outrage Spreads (13–14) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap your Partisan Seizing card (03) and clear all your Outrage.
Campaign/6/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/6/piece_3_1.jpg.description: Scrap this and clear all your Outrage if you're not the Partisan. You cannot seize the initiative by discarding a second action card. (This doesn't limit other ways to seize.) Instead, you may seize the initiative when you play a card if you choose to Provoke Outrage with a non-Outraged resource. (Discard resources and Guild cards of the Outrage type. Place an agent on the matching Outrage slot.) Remember, you can't seize the initiative if you have it or it has been seized!
Campaign/6/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Partisan Seizing 1

Campaign/6/piece_3_2.jpg.description: Spy (Influence): Once per turn, give your action card of highest number to a Rival. After you do, they must give you their action card of highest number. (It can be the same card.)
Campaign/6/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Informants

Campaign/6/piece_3_3.jpg.description:
Partisan – Act I.
1. Gain Informants (02) and Partisan Seizing (03).
Flip this card over.
Campaign/6/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/7/Fate7.jpg.description:
Restore the strength of the Empire's fleet and claim your rightful throne.

Your path to power:
I. Prove yourself by enlarging the Imperial fleet and using it in battle to take Trophies.
II. Exert Imperial control of planets and dominate your foes, Regents and Outlaws alike.
III. Control the Reach's planets and build the strongest navy.

Complexity: 1/3
Campaign/7/Fate7.jpg.name: Admiral

Campaign/7/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act III. Keep the Monopoly on Force. You're a Regent and...

<target> You control at least 9 planets.
<target> You have more Loyal ships on the map than each Rival.
» You control planets that are controlled by the Empire if you're the First Regent.
Campaign/7/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/7/back_1_2.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act II. <timer> 30.

Expand the Navy's Holdings. At the end of a chapter, if you're a Regent, advance <timer> 1 for each planet controlled by the Empire.

Crush the Empire's Foes. While you're a Regent, advance <timer> 2 for each Outlaw piece you take as a Trophy in battle.
» Unlike in Act I, you don't advance from Blight anymore!

Dominate Rival Regents. While you're a Regent, advance <timer> 2 for each Regent agent you capture.
» This doesn't count pieces transferred during Summits.
Campaign/7/back_1_2.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/7/back_2_6.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act I. <timer> 13.

Prove Yourself as a New Admiral. While you're a Regent and winning an ambition, advance <timer> 1 for each Imperial ship that anyone places on the map.
» Imperial ships are placed with the Use Imperial Foundries and Govern the Imperial Reach (Policy of Escalation) edicts.

Demonstrate the Navy's Might. While you're a Regent, advance <timer> 1 for each Trophy you take in battle with attacking Imperial ships.
» To advance, you can battle Blight, Free cities, and Outlaws.
» You can attack with both Loyal and Imperial ships, and the Imperial ship can be destroyed.
Campaign/7/back_2_6.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/7/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
Tuck this under the card play area. Regents except the Admiral follow these rules if they do not have Imperial Officers or Rogue Admirals:
» You cannot Command the Imperial Fleet.
» If you're the First Regent, you don't control Empire-controlled systems for ambitions, objectives, and events.
» When taking a Catapult move, Empire control of gates ends your move.
Campaign/7/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Martial Law 2

Campaign/7/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Regents except the Admiral follow these rules if they do not have Imperial Officers or Rogue Admirals:
» You cannot Command the Imperial Fleet. (On your turn, you don't control Empire-controlled systems and can't take actions with Imperial ships.)
» If you're the First Regent, you don't control Empire-controlled systems for ambitions, objectives, and events.
» When taking a Catapult move, Empire control of gates ends your move.
Campaign/7/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Martial Law 1

Campaign/7/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act III.
1. Add Martial Law (20) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
2. As a reminder, tuck the second copy of Martial Law (21) partially under the card play area of the map.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/7/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/7/piece_0_3.jpg.description: Crisis: Destroy every damaged ship. Bury this card. When Secured: Choose a cluster on the map. You place 1 ship in each system of that cluster. Discard this card.
Campaign/7/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Mass Uprising 2

Campaign/7/piece_0_4.jpg.description: Crisis: Destroy every damaged ship. Bury this card. When Secured: Choose a cluster on the map. You place 1 ship in each system of that cluster. Discard this card.
Campaign/7/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Mass Uprising 1

Campaign/7/piece_0_5.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may discard this card or resolve the Crisis in a cluster of your choice.
Campaign/7/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 4

Campaign/7/piece_0_6.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may discard this card or resolve the Crisis in a cluster of your choice.
Campaign/7/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 3

Campaign/7/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Scrap the 2 Imperial Defectors (15–16).
2. Add the 2 Mass Uprisings (17–18) to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Add the 2 Imperial Defectors (15–16) to the Court deck.
2. Scrap the 2 Mass Uprisings (17–18).
Campaign/7/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/7/piece_1_1.jpg.description: Crisis: Attach Imperial Officers here. Shuffle all Rogue Admirals from the Court discard pile into the Court deck. When Secured: Influence once. You may become a Regent. Shuffle this card into the Court deck.
Campaign/7/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Command Chaos

Campaign/7/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act II.
1. Shuffle Command Chaos (13) into the Court deck.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/7/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/7/piece_1_3.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may discard this card or resolve the Crisis in a cluster of your choice.
Campaign/7/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 2

Campaign/7/piece_1_4.jpg.description: Crisis: In the rolled cluster, replace all Imperial ships with ships of the Outlaw earliest in turn order. Bury this card. When Secured: You may discard this card or resolve the Crisis in a cluster of your choice.
Campaign/7/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Imperial Defectors 1

Campaign/7/piece_1_5.jpg.description: Abduct (Battle): Capture all Rival agents from a card in the Court that has fewer Rival agents than your total Weapon icons from resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/7/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Court Enforcers 2

Campaign/7/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Abduct (Battle): Capture all Rival agents from a card in the Court that has fewer Rival agents than your total Weapon icons from resources and cards. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 ships in a system you control.
Campaign/7/piece_1_6.jpg.name: Court Enforcers 1

Campaign/7/piece_2_0.jpg.description: Bury this if you're an Outlaw. You ignore the Empire's Presence, Movement, and Truce Laws. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 Imperial ships in a system you control.
Campaign/7/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Rogue Admirals 2

Campaign/7/piece_2_1.jpg.description: Bury this if you're an Outlaw. You ignore the Empire's Presence, Movement, and Truce Laws. Prelude: You may discard this to place 3 Imperial ships in a system you control.
Campaign/7/piece_2_1.jpg.name: Rogue Admirals 1

Campaign/7/piece_2_2.jpg.description: Your Weapon Guild cards cannot be stolen while you have any Weapons.
Campaign/7/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Honor Guard

Campaign/7/piece_2_3.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain Honor Guard (05).
2. Add the 2 Rogue Admirals (06–07) and 2 Court Enforcers (08–09) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap the 2 Imperial Defectors (10–11).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap the Use Imperial Foundries edict (03) from the rules booklet.
2. Scrap Honor Guard (05) and the 2 Rogue Admirals (06–07).
