3. Add the 2 Court Enforcers (08–09) and 2 Imperial Defectors (10–11) to the Court deck.
Campaign/7/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/7/piece_2_4.jpg.description: The player who has Imperial Officers resolves this. For each Loyal starport, you may replace 1 Loyal ship in its system with 1 Imperial ship. If you're the Admiral, remember to advance your objective if you're winning an ambition!
Campaign/7/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Use Imperial Foundries

Campaign/7/piece_2_5.jpg.description: Bury this if you are an Outlaw. You ignore the Empire's Presence and Movement Laws. (Basically, on your turn, you can take actions with any Imperial ships and you control all Empire-controlled systems! You don't need Loyal pieces in either case.)
Campaign/7/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Imperial Officers

Campaign/7/piece_2_6.jpg.description:
Admiral – Act I.
1. Gain Imperial Officers (02).
2. Add the Use Imperial Foundries edict (03) to the rules booklet.
3. Gain the First Regent tile unless the Steward is in play.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/7/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/8/Fate8.jpg.description:
Open their eyes to the Truth.

Your path to power:
I. Add Faithful action cards into play while winning ambitions.
II. Establish the Ideals of your faith by declaring ambitions with Faithful cards and fulfilling them.
III. Insist that the ambitions with Ideals are consistently declared.

Complexity: 3/3
Campaign/8/Fate8.jpg.name: Believer

Campaign/8/back_0_2.jpg.description:
Believer – Act III. Guide the Flock.

<target> Two ambition markers are on ambitions with Ideals.
<target> All three ambition markers are on ambitions with Ideals.
» This objective checks for how many ambition markers are on ambitions with Ideals, not how many different ambitions with Ideals have markers.
Campaign/8/back_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/8/back_1_2.jpg.description:
You must play Faithful action cards on their Wisdom side.

When you play a Faithful action card:
» You cannot declare ambitions this turn that have the Zeal Ideal.
» If you declare an ambition this turn with no Ideal, place an Ideal marker in its ambition box on the Wisdom side.

In Act III setup, this Doctrine card starts on the side matching the Ideal with more markers on ambition boxes. On a tie, it starts on Wisdom.
Campaign/8/back_1_2.jpg.name: Doctrine of Wisdom

Campaign/8/back_1_4.jpg.description:
Believer – Act II. <timer> 16–20 (2 players: 20 <timer> / 3 players: 18 <timer> / 4 players: 16 <timer>).

Develop the Ideals of your Faith. Each time you win an ambition that has an Ideal marker, advance <timer> 1 for each Power you gain from it.
» As described on Doctrinal Faith in the rules booklet, players place Ideals on ambitions they declare on turns where they play Faithful action cards.
» Count bonus Power from city slots uncovered on your player board. For example, if you gain 5 Power from winning an ambition with an Ideal marker and gain 5 bonus city Power, advance your objective marker 10 spaces.
Campaign/8/back_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/8/back_4_2.jpg.description:
Believer – Act I. <timer> 8–10 (2 players: 10 <timer> / 3 players: 9 <timer> / 4 players: 8 <timer>).

Spread the Good Word. Each time you win an ambition, advance <timer> 1 for each action pip on the top card of the Faithful action card stack. If the stack is empty, advance <timer> 10 instead.
» Using your Spreading the Faith card, you can attach Faithful action cards from their stack onto cards in the Court.
» If you win multiple ambitions in the same chapter, advance multiple times!
Campaign/8/back_4_2.jpg.name: Act I Objective

Campaign/8/piece_0_0.jpg.description: When a Rival of the Believer destroys a Loyal city of the Believer, the destroying player may remove 1 Ideal token from an undeclared ambition.
Campaign/8/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Breaking the Faith

Campaign/8/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Follow these same rules from before:
You must play Faithful action cards on the same side as the Doctrine.

When you play a Faithful action card, you must follow these rules on your turn when you want to declare an ambition.
» If the ambition box has no Ideal marker, place an Ideal marker showing the Doctrine in the box.
» If the ambition box does have an Ideal marker, you cannot declare this ambition if its Ideal doesn't match the Doctrine.

There is one new limit: When you play a non-Faithful card, you cannot declare an ambition with an Ideal this turn.
Campaign/8/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Dominant Faith

Campaign/8/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Believer – Act III.
1. Tuck Dominant Faith (30) in front of Doctrinal Faith (23) in the rules booklet. Explain Dominant Faith to everyone.
2. Add Breaking the Faith (31) to the rules booklet. Explain it to everyone.
3. Flip the Doctrine card (22) to match the Ideal with more markers on ambition boxes. On a tie, flip it to its Wisdom side.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/8/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/8/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Follow these same rules from before:
» You must play Faithful action cards on the same side as the Doctrine.
» When you play a Faithful action card you cannot declare ambitions that turn if they have an Ideal different from the Doctrine.

There is one new limit: If you play a Faithful action card, you cannot declare ambitions that have no Ideal this turn.

These rules don't limit declaring ambitions with non-Faithful cards.
Campaign/8/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Repressed Faith

Campaign/8/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Players reveal their Faithful action cards. The player who reveals the highest initiative number may change the Doctrine to Zeal or Wisdom, flipping the Doctrine card tucked into the card play area on the map.

The Doctrine determines the side that Faithful action cards must be played on, and which ambitions can be declared by leading Faithful action cards.
Campaign/8/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Set Lay Doctrine

Campaign/8/piece_0_5.jpg.description: When you declare Keeper or Empath, do not place the zero marker.
Campaign/8/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Secret Order 2

Campaign/8/piece_0_6.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard 1 non-Event action card to place this card next to a played Faithful card. (Even one that's face down!) When the round ends, draw it into your hand and take this card.
Campaign/8/piece_0_6.jpg.name: The Prophet

Campaign/8/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Believer – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

Record all Ideals on your record sheet.

If you completed your objective:
1. If you have The Young Light (16), scrap it and gain The Prophet (25). Otherwise, scrap The Prophet (25).
2. Scrap Secret Order (26), Set Lay Doctrine (27), and Repressed Faith (28).

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap all Ideal markers not on ambitions.
2. Scrap The Prophet (25).
3. Add Secret Order (26) to the Court deck.
4. Scrap Set Doctrine (21) and Doctrinal Faith (23) from the rules booklet, and add Set Lay Doctrine (27) and Repressed Faith (28) to it. Explain them to everyone.
Campaign/8/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/8/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
From now on, you must play Faithful action cards on the same side as the Doctrine, shown by the card tucked near the card play area.

When you play a Faithful action card, you must follow these rules on your turn when you want to declare an ambition.
» If the ambition box has no Ideal marker, place an Ideal marker showing the Doctrine in the box.
» If the ambition box does have an Ideal marker, you cannot declare this ambition if its Ideal doesn't match the Doctrine.

These rules do not change non-Faithful cards. The Ideals will remain in Act III.
Campaign/8/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Doctrinal Faith

Campaign/8/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
You must play Faithful action cards on their Zeal side.

When you play a Faithful action card:
» You cannot declare ambitions this turn that have the Wisdom Ideal.
» If you declare an ambition this turn with no Ideal, place an Ideal marker in its ambition box on the Zeal side.

In Act III setup, this Doctrine card starts on the side matching the Ideal with more markers on ambition boxes. On a tie, it starts on Wisdom.
Campaign/8/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Doctrine of Zeal

Campaign/8/piece_1_3.jpg.description: The player who holds The Young Light or The Prophet may change the Doctrine to Zeal or Wisdom, flipping the Doctrine card tucked into the card play area on the map. The Doctrine determines the side that Faithful action cards must be played on, and which ambitions can be declared by leading Faithful action cards.
Campaign/8/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Set Doctrine

Campaign/8/piece_1_4.jpg.description:
Believer – Act II.
1. Collect the 5 Ideal markers. (They are double-sided!)
2. Add the Set Doctrine edict (21) to rules booklet.
3. Tuck the Doctrine card (22) into the card play area on the left edge of the map. If you have the initiative, place it to show its Zeal side. If not, place it to show its Wisdom side.
4. Scrap Pluralistic Faith (14) from the rules booklet and add Doctrinal Faith (23) to it. Explain it to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/8/piece_1_4.jpg.name: Act II Setup

Campaign/8/piece_1_5.jpg.description: When you declare Keeper or Empath, do not place the zero marker.
Campaign/8/piece_1_5.jpg.name: Secret Order 1

Campaign/8/piece_1_6.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard 1 non-Event action card to place this card next to a played Faithful card. (Even one that's face down!) When the round ends, draw it into your hand and take this card.
Campaign/8/piece_1_6.jpg.name: The Prodigal One

Campaign/8/piece_2_0.jpg.description: Crisis: Attach The Young Light or The Prophet here. Each player discards their highest-number Faithful action card. When Secured: Search the action discard pile, draw the Faithful action card of highest number, then shuffle it. Discard Plot to Kidnap.
Campaign/8/piece_2_0.jpg.name: Plot to Kidnap

Campaign/8/piece_2_1.jpg.description: Prelude: You may discard 1 non-Event action card to place this card next to a played Faithful card. (Even one that's face down!) When the round ends, draw it into your hand and take this card.
Campaign/8/piece_2_1.jpg.name: The Young Light

Campaign/8/piece_2_2.jpg.description:
Believer – Act I. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Gain The Young Light (16).
2. Add Plot to Kidnap (17) to the Court deck.
3. Scrap your Spreading the Faith (03), The Prodigal One (18), and Secret Order (19).
4. Add all Faithful action cards left in their stack to the Court deck.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap your Spreading the Faith (03), The Young Light (16), and Plot to Kidnap (17).
2. Add The Prodigal One (18) and Secret Order (19) to the Court deck.
3. Add all Faithful action cards left in their stack to the Court deck.
Campaign/8/piece_2_2.jpg.name: Act I Resolution

Campaign/8/piece_2_3.jpg.description: When you Lead a Faithful action card, you may use it to declare any ambition. This works exactly like when you Lead a "7" action card.
Campaign/8/piece_2_3.jpg.name: Pluralistic Faith

Campaign/8/piece_2_4.jpg.description:
Faithful cards are a new suit of action cards. When you secure one from the Court, draw it into your hand. When you play one, place the card on either its Zeal or Wisdom side.
» Zeal lets you move, influence, battle, or tax
» Wisdom lets you build, repair, or secure

They have a couple differences from normal action cards:
» To Surpass a Faithful card, you must play a Faithful card on the same side. (So if a player leads Zeal, you only Surpass if you play Zeal. You can still Pivot to Wisdom.)
» You do not seize the initiative when you Surpass with a "7" Faithful action card.
Campaign/8/piece_2_4.jpg.name: Faithful Cards

Campaign/8/piece_2_5.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 1.
Campaign/8/piece_2_5.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 1

Campaign/8/piece_2_6.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 2.
Campaign/8/piece_2_6.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 2

Campaign/8/piece_3_0.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 3.
Campaign/8/piece_3_0.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 3

Campaign/8/piece_3_1.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 4.
Campaign/8/piece_3_1.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 4

Campaign/8/piece_3_2.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 5.
Campaign/8/piece_3_2.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 5

Campaign/8/piece_3_3.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 6.
Campaign/8/piece_3_3.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 6

Campaign/8/piece_3_4.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 7. (Leading a "7" can declare any ambition; Surpassing with a "7" does not seize the initiative.)
Campaign/8/piece_3_4.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 7

Campaign/8/piece_3_5.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 8.
Campaign/8/piece_3_5.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 8

Campaign/8/piece_3_6.jpg.description: Faithful Zeal (red side): Move or Influence or Battle or Tax. Faithful Wisdom (blue side): Build or Repair or Secure. Dual-suit Faithful action card, number 9.
Campaign/8/piece_3_6.jpg.name: Faithful Action Card 9

Campaign/8/piece_4_0.jpg.description: Teach (Influence): Scrap the bottom non-Event action card from the discard pile. (Continue past Events.) If you do, attach the top Faithful action card from its stack to a Guild card in the Court whose suit matches a planet with a Loyal city. Once you're familiar with this, you can tuck this card partially under your Faithful action card stack to save space.
Campaign/8/piece_4_0.jpg.name: Spreading the Faith

Campaign/8/piece_4_1.jpg.description: If you play a Faithful action card and declare an ambition you're winning, gain one more action pip.
Campaign/8/piece_4_1.jpg.name: Faithful Disciples

Campaign/8/piece_4_2.jpg.description:
Believer – Act I.
1. Gain Faithful Disciples (02) and Spreading the Faith (03).
2. Stack the 9 Faithful action cards (04–12) in number order so the "1" card is on the bottom and the "9" card is on the top. (These cards don't list their card numbers.)
3. Add Faithful Cards (13) and Pluralistic Faith (14) to the rules booklet. Explain them to everyone.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/8/piece_4_2.jpg.name: Act I Setup

Campaign/9/Fate9.jpg.description:
There's rumor of a Reach beyond our Reach. We must escape the decay engulfing us here.

Your path to power:
II. Search for clues and locate the secret Portal. Abandon your buildings for a Flagship.
III. Guide the Pilgrims to the Portal. Rivals can gain Power by drawing ships into the Portal.

Complexity: 2/3
Campaign/9/Fate9.jpg.name: Pathfinder

Campaign/9/back_0_4.jpg.description:
Pathfinder – Act III. See the Pilgrims Delivered.

<target> Control the Portal token.
<target> Have more Pilgrims on this card than the current Chapter.
» Your Pilgrims and the Portal card says how to move Pilgrims and get them on this card.
Campaign/9/back_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Objective

Campaign/9/back_1_3.jpg.description:
Pathfinder – Act II. <timer> 8.

Find the Portal.
Advance <timer> 1 each time a "Check" Clue token is placed. This cannot complete your objective.
Advance <timer> 8 when the Portal token is placed.
» Your Uncovering Clues ability card describes how Clues and the Portal are placed.
Campaign/9/back_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Objective

Campaign/9/piece_0_0.jpg.description:
Crisis: In turn order, each player must scrap 1 Loyal ship on the map.
When Secured: If you control the Portal token, scrap any number of any ships in its system. Gain 2 Power per ship scrapped. Shuffle this card into the Court deck.
Campaign/9/piece_0_0.jpg.name: Seek the Portal 3

Campaign/9/piece_0_1.jpg.description:
Crisis: In turn order, each player must scrap 1 Loyal ship on the map.
When Secured: If you control the Portal token, scrap any number of any ships in its system. Gain 2 Power per ship scrapped. Shuffle this card into the Court deck.
Campaign/9/piece_0_1.jpg.name: Seek the Portal 2

Campaign/9/piece_0_2.jpg.description:
Crisis: In turn order, each player must scrap 1 Loyal ship on the map.
When Secured: If you control the Portal token, scrap any number of any ships in its system. Gain 2 Power per ship scrapped. Shuffle this card into the Court deck.
Campaign/9/piece_0_2.jpg.name: Seek the Portal 1

Campaign/9/piece_0_3.jpg.description:
Pilgrim tokens are your Loyal pieces.
Prelude: Once per turn, you may move each Pilgrim token once into a system that you control or has your Flagship.
This is the only way Pilgrims can move. You cannot interrupt this Prelude action with other ones, like spending Fuel.
Use the Portal (Move): If you control the Portal token, take all Pilgrims from its system onto your objective card. Untuck a Seek the Portal card from here and place it on top of the Court deck.
Campaign/9/piece_0_3.jpg.name: Pilgrims

Campaign/9/piece_0_4.jpg.description:
Pathfinder – Act III.
1. Gain the Pilgrims card (08).
2. Place 1 Seek the Portal card (09) on top of the Court deck.
3. Tuck the other 2 Seek the Portal cards (10–11) under the Pilgrims and the Portal card (08).
4. Collect the 6 Pilgrim tokens. Roll the event die and place 1 Pilgrim token at each planet of the rolled symbol.
Flip this card over.
Campaign/9/piece_0_4.jpg.name: Act III Setup

Campaign/9/piece_0_5.jpg.description: In scoring, you fulfill a grand ambition if you control the Portal token. (Fulfilling three grand ambitions does not gain you more Power.)
Campaign/9/piece_0_5.jpg.name: Portal Seekers

Campaign/9/piece_0_6.jpg.description:
Crisis: If <triangle> was rolled, each player in turn order must discard 1 Guild card.

When Secured: Choose a suit of a Guild card you have. Clockwise from you, each Rival must discard all of their Guild cards of that suit. Shuffle Call to Pilgrimage into the Court deck.
Campaign/9/piece_0_6.jpg.name: Call to Pilgrimage

Campaign/9/piece_1_0.jpg.description:
Pathfinder – Act II. Wait to resolve this until the Intermission.

If you completed your objective:
1. Add Call to Pilgrimage (05) to the Court deck.
2. Scrap Portal Seekers (06).
3. Build 1 Upgrade or Armor on your Flagship board, ignoring the need to be at a specific planet.

If you failed your objective:
1. Scrap Call to Pilgrimage (05).
2. Add Portal Seekers (06) to the Court deck.
3. Place the Portal token on the system that was recorded during setup.
4. Scrap all Clue tokens, your Uncovering Clues card (02), and the Clues to the Portal card (03).
Campaign/9/piece_1_0.jpg.name: Act II Resolution

Campaign/9/piece_1_1.jpg.description:
The Pathfinder can make you place a Clue token at a planet. If you have none in your supply, take it from the map.
» Place the Clue on its "Check" side if the planet is either in the correct cluster or has the correct ID symbol (<triangle> / <hex> / <moon>).
» Place the Clue on its "X" side if the planet is not in the correct cluster and does not have the correct ID symbol (<triangle> / <hex> / <moon>).
» If their Flagship is at the correct planet, instead place the Portal token. Scrap all Clues, this card, and the Uncovering Clues card.

You can't discuss or hint at the Portal's system. You can only place these tokens as described.
Campaign/9/piece_1_1.jpg.name: Clues to the Portal

Campaign/9/piece_1_2.jpg.description:
Prelude: If your Flagship is at a planet with no Clue token, you may spend 1 resource that is different from all planets in its cluster to make the player to your right place 1 Clue token at the planet. Gain 1 resource of the planet type for each city there, and place 2 ships there for each starport there.
» A "Check" Clue means the planet is either in the right cluster or it has the correct ID symbol (<triangle> / <hex> / <moon>).
» An "X" Clue means the planet is not in the right cluster and does not have the correct ID symbol (<triangle> / <hex> / <moon>).
» A Portal token means that you found the Portal! (You complete your objective.)
Campaign/9/piece_1_2.jpg.name: Uncovering Clues

Campaign/9/piece_1_3.jpg.description:
Pathfinder – Act II.
1. You gain a Flagship—take a Flagship board and set it up as described on the board back.
2. Place 1 starport on your Slipstream Drive Upgrade. Place 1 city on your Hull Upgrade.
3. Gain the Uncovering Clues card (02).
4. The player to your right secretly writes down the cluster number and planet ID symbol (<triangle> / <hex> / <moon>) of any one planet on the map.
5. Give the Clues to the Portal card (03), 6 Clue tokens, and the Portal token to the player to your right. (The Clues are double-sided!)
Flip this card over.
Campaign/9/piece_1_3.jpg.name: Act II Setup
